title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Đạt Hoàng
text:
Nhà ga T3 Tân Sơn Nhất mang lại cảm giác hiện đại và thông thoáng hơn so với các nhà ga cũ. Không gian rộng, trần cao, ánh sáng tốt nên di chuyển khá dễ chịu, đặc biệt vào giờ cao điểm. Khu vực làm thủ tục được bố trí khoa học, bảng chỉ dẫn rõ ràng, dễ tìm quầy check-in và cổng ra máy bay.

Điểm cộng là thiết kế mới nên nhìn sạch sẽ, gọn gàng; lối đi rộng, ít cảm giác chật chội. Các khu vệ sinh và khu chờ cũng khá ổn, đủ chỗ ngồi, không quá ồn ào. Nhân viên hỗ trợ nhìn chung thân thiện và xử lý thủ tục nhanh.

Tuy nhiên, do còn khá mới nên một số khu dịch vụ ăn uống và mua sắm chưa thật sự đa dạng. Lối kết nối giao thông bên ngoài vào giờ cao điểm vẫn có thể đông, cần thêm thời gian để quen đường.

Tổng thể, T3 là một bước nâng cấp đáng ghi nhận của sân bay Tân Sơn Nhất: hiện đại hơn, tiện nghi hơn và trải nghiệm tốt hơn so với trước. Hy vọng trong thời gian tới, các dịch vụ được hoàn thiện thêm để đáp ứng tốt hơn lượng khách ngày càng đông.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: O Olive “Lily”
text:
Tan Son Nhat International Airport is a fairly small and simple airport compared to other major hubs in Asia. When I transited from Japan to Vietnam, I had to pass through another small immigration checkpoint, and it was very crowded with long lines, so the process felt a bit slow and congested. The space inside can get packed quickly, especially during busy hours.

On the plus side, there are some decent food options. I had a bowl of pho inside the airport that was actually really good and comforting after a flight. The only downside is the price — about $21 for pho and $6 for an orange juice, which feels pretty expensive for Vietnam. Overall it’s functional and gets the job done, but don’t expect a super modern or spacious airport experience.

Mong muốn xem xét lại cách bảo vệ sân bay làm việc chứ không thể chấp nhận cái cách đối xử bất công như thế. Mong muốn mọi người nước ngoài khác đến sẽ không bị trường hợp như tôi!

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Thanh Tú Phan
text:
1. Vị trí và tiếp cận
Sân bay Tân Sơn Nhất nằm rất gần trung tâm TP HCM so với nhiều sân bay lớn khác. Điều này giúp tiết kiệm thời gian di chuyển và tiền taxi hoặc Grab so với những sân bay xa hơn. Giao thông xung quanh vào giờ cao điểm có thể khá đông xe nhưng vẫn tiện lợi cho đa số hành khách.

2. Quy mô và cơ sở vật chất
Khu vực nhà ga quốc nội và quốc tế được tách biệt rõ ràng. Nhà ga quốc nội khá gọn hơn nhiều so với nhà ga quốc tế và phù hợp với lượng khách nội địa. Nhà ga quốc tế rộng rãi hơn nhưng đôi lúc vẫn cảm giác hơi chật vào giờ cao điểm. Trần nhà cao, ánh sáng và bảng chỉ dẫn nhìn chung dễ theo dõi. Một số khu vực sân bay đã được nâng cấp trong vài năm gần đây giúp không gian sáng sủa hơn.

3. Thủ tục và trải nghiệm check-in
Check-in tự động và quầy nhân viên đều có sẵn. Hành khách nhận xét rằng thủ tục nhập cảnh/ kiểm tra an ninh thường khá nhanh vào buổi sáng và đầu giờ chiều nhưng có thể mất thời gian vào giờ cao điểm chiều tối. Nhân viên an ninh và hải quan nhìn chung lịch sự và hỗ trợ hành khách.

4. Dịch vụ và tiện ích
Sân bay có đủ dịch vụ cơ bản như quầy ăn uống, quầy cà phê, cửa hàng mua sắm, cửa hàng duty free ở khu quốc tế. Giá đồ ăn và nước uống trong sân bay thường cao hơn bên ngoài. Wi-Fi miễn phí hoạt động tương đối ổn nhưng đôi khi mất kết nối ở một số khu vực.

5. Nhà vệ sinh và môi trường chung
Nhà vệ sinh được dọn khá sạch trong suốt ngày nhưng khi lượng khách đông có lúc bị quá tải một chút. Sân bay không quá ồn ào nhưng mức độ đông đúc ở sảnh chờ, cửa ra tàu bay vẫn rõ rệt vào giờ cao điểm.

6. Đón tiễn và bãi đậu xe
Khu vực đón tiễn có nhiều phương tiện di chuyển như taxi, xe công nghệ. Bãi đậu xe khá rộng nhưng giá phí chưa hẳn rẻ nếu đậu lâu ngày. Vị trí bãi đậu xe cũng hơi xa so với cửa vào chính nếu phải đi bộ với hành lý nặng.

7. An toàn và cảm giác tổng thể
Sân bay an ninh tốt, có nhân viên hỗ trợ khách. Cảm giác tổng thể là sân bay hoạt động hiệu quả với lượng khách rất lớn mỗi ngày. So với các sân bay quốc tế khác trong khu vực, Tân Sơn Nhất không quá hiện đại nhưng vẫn đầy đủ chức năng và khá tiện lợi nếu bạn sắp xếp thời gian đúng giờ.

Tổng kết nhanh
Ưu điểm lớn nhất là vị trí gần trung tâm, thủ tục khá trôi chảy, dịch vụ đầy đủ. Nhược điểm là khu vực có thể đông vào giờ cao điểm, một vài tiện ích chưa đồng bộ hoàn toàn như ở các sân bay quá hiện đại khác. Nói chung đây là sân bay quốc tế hoạt động tốt dành cho đa số hành khách trong nước và quốc tế.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Rendra Regen Rais
text:
Not recommended for transit.

I had a connected flight with VietJet and still had to wait almost 3 hours just to get my transfer boarding pass. The transfer process is extremely inefficient, poorly organized, and not passenger-friendly at all.

Staff were unhelpful, unfriendly, and rigid, especially when a previous boarding pass was missing, even though all booking details were in the system. There is little to no clear guidance for transit passengers, and everything feels manual, slow, and stressful.
This airport is not designed as a proper transit hub. Overcrowding, long queues, lack of clear communication, and a cold attitude from staff make the experience exhausting.

If you have other options, avoid transiting here, especially with low-cost or connected flights.

Shoutout to the short-haired staff in security clearance for pushing me when I accidentally went in with my headphones on. Try to use your words next time.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Joseph
text:
Vietnam as a country was enjoyable, but everything completely fell apart at the airport. The nightmare started at the check-in counter. One staff member treated us with obvious disrespect and looked down on us. Despite the fact that we had already purchased extra baggage weight, she did not properly check our ticket and treated us as if we were trying to steal extra kilograms. This was humiliating and deeply unprofessional.

The situation only got worse with the officers. Instead of doing their jobs efficiently, they were clearly wasting time and deliberately slowing down the process. These delays felt intentional, creating unnecessary chaos so that passengers would feel pressured to purchase VIP Fast Track services. This kind of behavior is unethical and completely unacceptable.

Even after paying for Fast Track, we still waited nearly one hour in long queues. This makes the service feel like a scam rather than a premium service.

Our flight departure time was 12:05, yet passengers were only allowed to pass the boarding gate until 11:15. Even though the aircraft had not departed, anyone arriving after that time was simply rejected with no explanation, no flexibility, and no concern for passengers.

The customer service from start to finish was awful. Disrespectful staff, poor management, no accountability, and zero professionalism. My experience with Vietravel Airlines and this airport was beyond disappointing. I strongly warn other travelers to be extremely careful or avoid this airline and airport altogether unless serious changes are made.
Even one star they’re not dessert.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Aditya

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: 松岡啓祐
text:
ソウル→ダナン→サイゴン→セブ→マニラ→福岡に帰って来ております。
何故こうなったかと言うと私の情報収集能力不足＆事前確認能力不足でベトナムからフィリピンにオンラインチェックイン出来ずチェックインカウンターで初めてフィリピン入国の際には第三国へ出国する航空券が必要で空港カウンターで「いまここで買いなさい」と焦らされフィリピンの次の国をゆっくり考えているひまは無くマニラ福岡という安易な帰国便をスマホで検索して買ってしまったからです。
今から思うとすぐ隣の台湾行きのチケットを検索してみても良かった！
もう何度も行ったことあるという先入観と搭乗時刻までのぎり時間との戦いをエンジョイできる器の大きさが私にはまだなかったー！
でもこれで一度経験したので次回似たような事態になったとき片目を閉じてねゔぁ〜と指クネしながら粘エンジョることができるでしょう！
セキュリティ後はお店も飲食店も数は多くない。
出発ロビーの入り口手前のドミノピザがおいしかった。
保安検査場で靴を脱ぎ忘れていたら身体の大きな(失礼)女性検査官に怒られた！

name: Queen Cee
text:
This review is for LOST and FOUND COUNTER at Arrival in Tan Son Nhat Airport.
We flew from the US to HCMC using American Airlines and Japan Airlines.

The flight from the US with AA delayed, which caused us to miss the connection with Japan Airlines to Haneda airport to go to HCMC
At O’hare airport, AA agent was great on helping us to book another flight, so we arrived one day later, but our luggages didn’t arrive.

We went to Lost Luggage counter and were assisted immediately by Hoang and Dat, after filling the form, Hoang and Dat asked some questions to confirm the brands and the colors of the luggages, they gave us the paper works and informed us that they will follow up promptly.
Not even 2 minutes passed, we received an email apology from Tan Son Nhat airport with details of our luggage and the claim numbers.
The next day, we received another email and a phone call to inform us that the luggages were on the way to the HCMC and they will call us.
The next morning, we received a phone call to informed us that the luggages will be delivered to our address in 45 minutes.
What a GREAT services!
In the mean time, there was no follow up nor apology from American Airlines nor from Japan Airlines.
Big applause to the Lost & Found Team at TSN airport, special shout out to Hoang and Dat who were so amazing with their top notch services. Btw, their English is very good too.
Thank You for such a great service, relieved us from the stressful situation.

name: Dave Chik
text:
Airport of government corruption. I was one of the people who filled in some information wrong for my visa.

Immigration demanded $120 USD. A Taiwanese woman next to me filled in her passport number wrong - was asked for $250USD. A third person put dual nationality wrongly, was asked for $2000USD.

No pricing menu, no signage mentioning of the above penalty. Just accept whatever price they request from you or go back to your home country.

Fun part? You can negotiate the price to a lower one, and conveniently if you dont have cash? There is an ATM right next to the counter for you to get cash.

I asked for an official receipt for reimbursement as i was on business trip - none. They do not have and will not offer.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: John Christian Bryan Velasco
text:
Worst experience in an airport ever! Been to Japan, Philippines, Hong Kong, Singapore, South Korea and the big cities in the United States but nothing compared to this airport.

We already got a heads up on the long queue for immigration so we were prepared for that, but what we did not expect was the rudeness of the staff especially that short-haired lady from the domestic security checkpoint who pushed my mother.

We came here and stayed for 1 night in Ho Chi Minh before heading to Da Nang via air. We get to experience the domestic part of this airport and it was a nightmare. When we came in, a lady was shouting at the Vietjet stuff about something we don’t understand because she was speaking in her native language. We somehow made speculations about why she’s mad and been judging her for her outburst. Little did we know that we will get to understand why later.

The cue for check-in to Vietjet was long and kinda slow. When it was our turn, it takes a little longer than usual since there’s 6 of us (supposed to be 7 but ditched the last moment. He already had the ticket as well). After then, we received the boarding pass which was just a piece of paper (small, yet petty right? But it keeps adding on). Vietjet staff told us to wait for the checked in bag to clear but even after 10 mins of standing there, no one told us that the bag was cleared so we just left and went straight to the security clearance. Then another thing happened. While heading up the escalator, the Vietjet staff told us that we need to weigh our carry on bag, rudely. So we went there to the area and behold, they were weighing both carry on luggage and personal items together, even your phone and stuff in your pocket! We complied. All of our luggages were cleared for the 7kg but sadly 2 of us exceeded the limit because our personal carry on has all our medication, gadgets, notebooks and pens for monitoring our business schedules were there. We just told them that we’ll pay the extra fee for it. I mean it’s our fault for not knowing that rule but the problem we have is their attitudes. They were rude and raising voices at us and can’t even explain calmly what the problem is about. We’re here for a vacation so regardless of the problems that may come, we were just trying to be calm at it and not let it affect our mood but MAN, that was rough. I even saw one of them berating a foreign couple with her voice raised (they look like they’re of Indian descent, just an assumption). I’m not sure if that’s how they were trained or what but they need to do better.

Finally, after all that spectacle, we went up and head to the security checkpoint. We were still shaken by what we experienced so we were trying to joke it out even tho we were furious about how they handled that. Then my mom was stopped because she forgot to get rid of her headphones that was just resting on her neck. She wasn’t able to get rid of it because she was the one that was mostly angered by the event earlier. This was the short haired girl that pushed my Mom instead of using her mouth to tell her about it. She was a bully and fortunately for her, I was not able to see it on the act or else I would smack her face out. My mom told us about the incident when we were in the waiting area. That was the last straw on why I’m making this review. This just happened moments ago and we’re currently on board to Da Nang.

Please be careful with the “fixed-price taxi / transfer counters located immediately to the left after exiting the arrival lobby.”
I was charged 516,000 VND, which is nearly three times the normal fare to District 1.

In addition, the driver later demanded a non-existent “airport fee” of US$5, which I refused.
This taxi-like service added unexpected extra charges, making the experience very unpleasant.

For reference, on the return trip, a car arranged by my hotel to the airport cost only 143,000 VND.
At the airport, I strongly recommend using a metered taxi or ride-hailing apps such as Grab.

到着ロビーを出てすぐ左手にある「定額タクシー／送迎カウンター」には注意してください。
District 1まで通常相場の約3倍にあたる 516,000 VND を請求され、支払ってしまいました。

さらにドライバーから、実在しない 「空港手数料（US$5）」 を後出しで要求されましたが、これは拒否しました。
タクシーを装った商売で、後出し請求があり非常に不快でした。

参考までに、帰りはホテル手配の車で空港まで 143,000 VND でした。
空港ではメータータクシー、または Grab などの配車アプリの利用を強くおすすめします。

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Akuamari
text: This review is to help travelers get an understanding of the layout of the airport and how long it takes to get from place to place. My 2 week trip in Vietnam consisted of visiting 4 cities. I flew from city to city on Vietnam Airlines and each flight had about a 30 minute delay. On the day of departure, my first flight from Nha Trang to Ho Chi Minh City arrived at T3 but I needed to go to T2 for my connecting international flight. To get from T3 to T2, you need to go to the transfer counter, which includes a quick security screen, and take the elevator to F1. Upon exiting the elevator, you enter a lobby to wait for the free shuttle which takes you from T3 to T1 (there is no direct shuttle from T3 to T2). The shuttle comes every 20 minutes and the ride itself is less than 10 minutes. After the shuttle drops you off at T1, you completely exit T1 and turn left and follow signs to go to T2 (it’s about a 5 minute walk). Once you enter T2, you then have to take the escalator to F2 for departures. There, you go through immigration and then security. It took me a total of 50 minutes from when my plane touched down at T3 until I made it to T2 (the 50 minutes included the time to deplane, take shuttle #1 from the plane to T3, transfer and go through round 1 of security, take shuttle #2 from T3 to T1, walk from T1 to T2, take the escalator to level 2 for departures). Immigration line is very long and very slow. I paid for the fast track ($30 on Klook) and got through immigration and security in 15 minutes. If I hadn’t gotten the fast track, I’m 100% certain I would’ve missed my connecting flight despite the original 2 hour and 45 minute layover.

No soap in any of the restrooms is a DISGRACE for an international airport. (This was true of both Tan Son Nhat International Airport and Phu Quoc International Airport...)

So many diseases are easily preventable via hand-washing, especially the diarrhea-related illnesses that SE Asia is notorious for, as well as many respiratory infections like colds and the flu. The last thing the world needs is more people transmitting diseases across the globe because an airport cannot be bothered to pay for soap refills! Soap is cheap!!! Do better, Vietnam.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: rumah cantik Amelia
text:
Thực ra tôi lười viết một câu chuyện như thế này, nhưng tôi thực sự rất khó chịu.
Chuyện xảy ra tại Cổng số 4 Sân bay Quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất trên đường tôi đến Jakarta.
Tôi bị lừa tại một cửa hàng tiện lợi ở Sân bay Quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất.
Tôi không kịp chụp ảnh cửa hàng tiện lợi; nó nằm ngay trước Cổng số 4.
Họ bán bằng USD, nên tôi đã yêu cầu thu ngân đổi sang tiền Việt Nam.
Tổng số tiền tôi mua là 1.260.000. Điều này rất kỳ lạ vì tôi chỉ mua đồ ăn vặt cho bọn trẻ, mà cũng không đắt lắm. Khi ngồi xuống, tôi kiểm tra lại.
Hóa ra tất cả các nhãn giá đã bị gỡ bỏ.
Tôi quay lại cửa hàng tiện lợi và yêu cầu xuất trình hóa đơn. Tôi cũng chụp ảnh lại giá, nhưng nhân viên VET Jet đã gọi tôi lên xe buýt rồi.
Vì vậy, tôi đã tính toán lại trên xe buýt đến máy bay. Quả nhiên, tổng tiền tôi mua chỉ có 24 đô la, vậy mà thu ngân lại đòi đến 1.260.000 đô la. Thật đáng tiếc khi lại có những kẻ lừa đảo như thế này ở các cửa hàng tiện lợi trong Sân bay Quốc tế Hồ Chí Minh.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Begüm
text:
Aufgrund der vielen negativen Bewertungen sind wir 3 std früher angereist. Bei den Domestic flights ist es nicht so schlimm. Es was relativ leer, auch die Passkontrolle.

Was jedoch sehr unverschämt ist, ist das Bodenpersonal bei der Kofferabgabe (Vietjet!)- wir hatten 3 aufgebende Gepäckstücke und diese davor auch gewogen. Die Waageanzeige war ausgeblendet und hat nicht funktioniert. Beim letzten Gepäckstück meinte sie dann ihr habt 3 kilo zuviel.. das war enorm irritierend und beim letzten Gepäckstück habe ich dann auch hinter dem Schalter geschaut , dass sie mir das bitte zeigen soll. Da war unser letzter Koffer dann auf 19kg und sie meinte, die davor haben mehr gewogen. Als wir nochmals 1-2 Jacken auspackten, weil die da davorigen Koffer schon weg waren, diskutierte wir mit ihr.
mein Mann packte unsere eigene Kofferwaage aus und wog vor ihren Augen den letzten Koffer. Wir haben sie ausdrücklich darauf hingewiesen, dass das eig nicht sein kann und wir wissen was die anderen wogen.

Das Gespräch ging dann tatsächlich nicht lang und sie war nicht mehr so beharrlich.

Fazit: Achtung aufpassen und nicht abzocken lassen!!! Wiegt eure Koffer und zahlt nicht unnötig Geld!

text:
You should plan to arrive at this airport at least 6 hours in advance if you really want to make sure you’re not late to your gate or want a moment to breathe. The airport staff is extremely rude, and most of them bearly speak any English. “Baggage inspection” apparently means that the airport staff has destroyed your suitcase. Metal conveyor belt versus plastic suitcase — guess who wins?

This is the most chaotic, most unfriendly, and most uncomfortable airport of any major city on planet, with no places where you can actually relax. This airport is pure stress!

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Kristan Ploughman
text: I'm a disabled frequent flyer. I've been to so many places. I have no choice but to use wheelchair assistance to get me to my plane. People don't see my cane and often knock into me. There is so much unself awareness.  I checked in with Vietnam airlines and was parked well away from where I checked in and promptly ignored. I don't mind 5, 10, 20 minutes, but it was down to the wire and I was ready to attempt everything myself. Finally a smug staff member arrived to get me to my plane. I felt like cattle.  Any able bodied passenger would be able to stop at duty free or go to a lounge to wait for their plane. The staff member didn't even attempt to interact with me, language barrier or not, a comforting smile goes a long way. I will avoid this airport in the future. Perhaps some lessons from Hanoi or Siem Reap are in order. Do better. We are not an inconvenience,  we are consumers.

text:
Nach den ganzen Horrorstories möchte ich gerne meine Erfahrung teilen. Abflug nach Dubai.
Wir waren knappe 3 Stunden früher da. Fluggesellschaft Emirates um 23:55 an einem Samstag.
Check in der Koffer 30 Minuten. Immigration hat nochmal 30 Minuten gedauert. Ja die Schlangen sind lang, aber es geht zügig. Auch laufen hier zwischendrin Leute der verschiedenen Fluggesellschaften herum, um Gäste rauszuziehen, die knapp dabei sind.
Security war dann nochmal 40 Minuten, aber dann hat man auch schnell danach sein Gate gefunden.
Ganz entspannt und massig Zeit hatten wir nicht, aber es war auch nicht super hektisch.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Google account
text:
Wow what an experience. Was it the hour long check in wait? The 2 hour! Long immigration wait? Who knows. All I know is this airport is not functional, whether its the snail like pace of the lines the obviously overworked staff, or the unused auto check in? I mean what’s the point of having an e-visa, a working passport and no checked luggage, you’re gonna wait anyways. Whole lines of people just desolate, and yet a dozen completely empty auto check in lines just staring at you, almost taunting you personally I think there just for show. And guess what…. You’re not even at security yet! Disorganised, randomised lines with no direction, just hilariously bad I thought my 5 hour early arrival was a bit much, I barely made it in time to my plane. The only way to get through this airport at a reasonable time is in a wheelchair. So if anyone wants to make some bucks in my knee smashing business, you’ll find my outside Tan  Son Nhat international airport.

Ps: only reason I didn’t take photos is because I want no memory of this place.

Edit: I wrote this in the line for the plane so I have more now that I’m on the plane, although I guess this is more criticism of the airline VietJet. How about another 30 minute wait on the bus for seemingly no reason? Don’t mind if I do. No seating on the bus, tightly packed even while all standing, once it finally got moving, it went what? 300 metres? And then once outside the plane we waited another 15, just to see another bus pull up, wait 10 more minutes, and 3 people got off that bus and got on. And then Finally! I was on the plane, I feel like I’m hallucinating. I think next time I’m gonna swim here, because I’d prefer the risk of drowning or being eaten by shark which would atleast happen in a timely matter. It’s like they don’t want you too leave, maybe that’s the point. It’s not the destination it’s the agonising pisstake of a journey trying to leave that makes it all worth it.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Mic
text:
"I would give Tan Son Nhat International  Airport Vietnam 1 star out of 5 due to the extremely disappointing experience I had during my recent visit. The service at the counter was subpar, with staff displaying a notably unw welcoming demeanor that was both surprising and unpleasant.

My experience was marred by excessive wait times and unhelpful responses from the staff. Specifically, I waited over an hour to collect my bags, and when I inquired about their whereabouts, I was met with a dismissive and uncooperative attitude. The staff member's response of 'I don't know, go ask someone else' was not only unhelpful but also unacceptable.

In total, I spent an additional 4 hours at the airport due to these issues, which was frustrating and avoidable. The lack of clear communication and support from the airport staff made an already stressful travel experience even more challenging.

Regrettably, I will not be returning to this country would caution others about the inadequate service. It is essential for airports to provide a higher level of service and care for their passengers, especially after a long and tiring journey. Unfortunately, Vietnam airport fell significantly short of these expectations, and I would not recommend it to others."

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Bhimesh Bandreddi
text:
I’ve recently had some challenging experiences traveling through Vietnam as an Indian tourist, and I’d like to share them constructively in the hope that things can improve for everyone.

While Vietnam actively promotes itself as a welcoming tourist destination, the reality at the airports often falls short of expectations. Immigration queues can take 2–3 hours even during off-peak times, boarding-pass counters move very slowly, and traffic management around the airports remains chaotic. For international flights, arriving 5 hours early is unfortunately not an exaggeration—it’s often necessary. These operational issues create a great deal of stress for all passengers, regardless of nationality.

At the same time, I must admit that some Indian travelers contribute to the negative perception. Occasionally, a few individuals disregard queues, speak loudly, or argue with staff, and these isolated incidents unfairly color how the entire group is viewed. As Indians, we often feel embarrassed when the actions of a small minority reflect poorly on the whole community abroad.

On the Vietnamese side, I’ve noticed that security and immigration staff sometimes appear more lenient toward Western tourist, which can feel discriminatory to travelers from South Asia. A more consistent and respectful approach toward every nationality would go a long way in improving the experience.

Both sides have room for improvement: Vietnamese authorities could streamline airport processes and train staff to treat all visitors equally, while we Indian travelers should be more mindful of local rules and courteous in public spaces. Only then can tourism in Vietnam truly live up to its warm and hospitable reputation.

I still believe Vietnam has incredible beauty and culture to offer. With better systems and a little more understanding from visitors, it can become a much more enjoyable destination for everyone.

Bác nào thiên tài mới chọn đầu tư vào cái hệ thống XNC thời tiền sử này. Lạy một ngàn lạy, thông minh nhất thế giới!

Arriving to the airport, the shuttle bus ride took about 15 minutes for some reason because it stopped multiple times.

On the plus side, we had no problems at any of these stations and in addition, there was staff that webt around the lines asking for people who were tight on schedule

text: Tan Son Nhat International Airport (SGN) in Ho Chi Minh City is the busiest and largest airport in Vietnam. It serves as a major hub for both domestic and international flights. It is very crowded. Long queues for the airline check-in and security check. You have to remove your shoes too for the security check. Come at least 3 to 4 hours before your flight to make sure you do not miss it. Luggage storage available outside the international arrival hall, between Pillar 13 and 14, beside Burger King. 27,000VND per hour, 270,000VND for 10 to 24 hours.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Masaki
text:
非常に不快な経験をしました。
まず、到着ロビーには「クレジットカードが使える」と言いながらも旅行者を騙すタクシー業者が大量におり、最終的に高額の現金を要求されます。空港職員も取り締まる様子はないです。
さらに、ベトジェット利用時のチェックイン手続きが異常に遅く、全てが手作業。オンラインチェックインが機能しておらず、15時に到着したにもかかわらず、ボーディングタイム（17:15）ぎりぎりまで列に並ばされました。
極めつけは、順番を早めるためにチップを渡すのが平然と行われていることです。空港という公共の場で賄賂が横行していることに衝撃を受けました。
また、この空港が「日本とベトナムの協働出資」で建設されたと紹介されていますが、運営の実態は日本の丁寧で効率的な仕事ぶりとは程遠く、明らかに冒涜しています。
旅行者の方は、空港内外のトラブルに十分注意してください。

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Bearandbug
text:
Absolutely discussed with the way we were treated at this airport Saturday 1st Nov... arriving at the airport on a domestic flight from Hanoi and the plane landed at/near the international airport on the tarmac not on a entrance shute ( when there was so many free at  terminal 3 )  because they wanted to clean and prepare the plane for a international flight to India.
So we stepped out of the exit ladder into a foot of water to get into a bus. Then was driven all the way to the domestic terminal 3 to then get put through transfer in the terminal there to get back on a bus another terminal, where we were dropped off and had to walk 10 mins through the terminal to check our bags again, then having to find the international terminal, to walk into a ants nest of people at immigration, wait another 1/2 hr here then our bags check AGAIN after this .. OMG a rigmaroll having our bags checked 3 times in 2 hrs and only just getting to our plane with 1/2 hr to spare... no time to relax before a 8 hr flight ...
Never want to do that airport ever again 😪

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Lêê Thịị Hồngg Vânn
text:
Tôi là người Việt Nam, nhưng tôi sẽ đánh giá cho cán bộ kiểm soát xuất nhập cảnh 0 Sao.⭐️ thái độ thì hống hách và tự cao.
Mình đọc bình luận thấy mọi người cho đây là sân bay tệ nhất thế giới. Nhưng mình thiết nghĩ, cán bộ kiểm soát xuất nhập cảnh làm cho hình ảnh sân bay và đất nước xấu đi phần nào.
Mỗi lần về Việt Nam đến đoạn kiểm tra hộ chiếu là mình sợ. Chưa có một đất nước nào mà bước qua cửa nhập cảnh lại kéo dài 3 tiếng như Việt Nam. 3 tiếng nhập cảnh là một điều không thể tưởng được, khi bạn đã bay một chặng đường dài 10000km rất mệt.
Đến lượt mình thì mình mới hiểu vì sao lại chậm như vậy. Chị ngân viên vừa gọi video call, vừa nhắn tin, trong lúc đang làm việc. Thái độ thì khó chịu vô cùng và rất tắc trách. Còn những hàng khác thì nhanh hơn. Chứng tỏ họ có thể làm việc mà, nhưng họ có muốn hay không thôi.
Mình cũng đi nhiều nơi, nhưng việt nam là nước duy nhất mình thấy phải tháo giày dép. Và Sài Gòn là nơi duy nhất đến đoạn ra khỏi cửa phải quét hành lý thêm một nữa. Hành lý khá nặng và nhiều người nhưng chỉ có một quầy nhỏ hoạt động nên lại phải đợi. Nhân viên thì gào quát nhanh lên cho người sau lên. Hành lý thì nhiều, đến đoạn này họ cùng đuối sức mọi thứ không thể làm nhanh nhưng nhân viên thì đứng gào gào quát quát.
Cái đoạn check nhập cảnh chúng tôi cũng đứng yên đợi đến lượt chứ có gào gào với cái chị nhân viên là chị ơi đừng gọi Video call nữa mà hãy làm việc đi đâu…
Nói chúng lợi bất cập hại. Những người làm ở đây chắc cũng con ông cháu cha nên muốn làm bố thiên hạ. Nên có nói nữa thì cũng không thấu đến trời cao. Nhưng tức quá vẫn nói ra cho nhẹ lòng tý. Và chắc nhiều người cũng cùng hoàn cảnh với mình quá.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Carles Adelantado Faura
text: Unfortunately, I will have to agree to the title of one of the worst airports in the world (not me, this was published recently). I cannot comprehend how in 2025 the immigration lines are so long and inefficient, especially when Saigon has become a major tourist city. All in all, terrible long queues (not uncommon for people to miss flights on the way out), rather rude officers that don’t care much about speed, ridiculous prices on food options (even prices in USD to make things more expensive). With the construction of the new airport… I have not much faith… I hope they prove me wrong. Of course, you can skip the lines if you pay a fee… so called VIP services… why do we need to pay for an efficient system? Singapore, Malaysia, Indonesia… they have optimized their immigration systems.

text: Ga đi quốc tế dịch vụ tệ, vệ sinh không sạch, các khâu đựng hành lý kiểm tra an ninh sôi chiếu thì  bẩn, không đc vệ sinh, không có nước suối miễn phí cho khách. Có một bình lọc nước ở gần cửa 15 mà không hoạt động được. Giá nước suối ở các quầy thì đắt 40,000vnd/chai 500ml. Bán mắt gấp 10 lần giá trị sản phẩm.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Christian Mallari
text: This airport is the exact same design as NAIA T3 so probably the same firm that made this airport. You should arrive at least 4 hours before your flight because that's what we did luckily and we had only 40 min to spare to get some food and coffee. The food and drinks inside the airport are super expensive and priced in USD instead of VND. If you spent 75 VND on something outside expect to pay at least 3x to 4x that. It's ridiculously overpriced. And inside the airport you don't have a choice because everything is priced that way. It's a total money grab. Anyways we tried to buy food and coffee from highlands coffee there near gate 23 and this is how it went. Sorry no visa card. No card only cash. Ok do you accept VND? Yes. Ok grabs some cash from the wife and come back. Ok I want to order coffee and food. Ok. Here is my cash. Sorry we are closed. Unbelievable! Also pay attention because they will keep changing your gate for the plane. Also no place to charge your phones. You might get lucky if there is an unattended plug near the waiting area.

name: Lars
text: The line at the immigration to exit the country is extremely long and slow. But the worst part is that they need the boarding pass printed and they only tell you after standing in line for an hour so you'll have to go back to the check-in desk to get the boarding pass and then queue again. Absolutely ridiculous practice, I fly with only hand luggage all the time and never needed that!

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: MH Tham
text:
A small airport with limited seating inside. Customs clearance is notably slow, even when using the express queue where you have to pay a top-up.

Food and beverages in the check-in area are priced in USD and is much more expensive than expected, reminiscent of a tourist trap. Some restaurant staff actively solicit customers near the dining area, which is quite intrusive.

Overall, the airport has considerable room for improvement before it can be considered satisfactory.

name: Nguyen Pham Hong Dao
text:
- Nhà vệ sinh sàn nhà tràn nước, hộp xà phòng rửa tay trống không, không một giọt.
- Khu chờ ở cổng lên máy bay chỉ có duy nhất một ổ điện còn dùng được. Còn lại đều lỏng lẽo mòn đến không thể giữ được cốc sạc.
- Máy châm nước thì 6-7 cổng mà chỉ được bố trí có 2 máy. Mà 1 cái lại còn bị HỎNG. Thậm chí không có cả nổi một cái cốc giấy.
Đây là đánh giá của tôi ở khu vực lên máy bay số 9, nhà ga 2, 7h sáng ngày 30/10/2025.
Sân bay là bộ mặt, là ấn tượng đầu tiên và cuối cùng của một người khi đến đất nước ta. Là một người Việt Nam, tôi rất mong sân bay sớm có biện pháp cải thiện các vấn đề tương tự như trên. Chứ nếu là người nước ngoài chắc tôi cũng chỉ cười khinh bỉ hả hê cho sự yếu kém của một đất nước thôi.

text:
Gemini
Gemini との会話
タンソンニャット国際空港

の説明

タンソンニャット国際空港についてですね。

タンソンニャット国際空港は、ベトナムのホーチミン市にある主要な国際空港です。

所在地: ベトナム社会主義共和国 ホーチミン市

特徴: ベトナムで最も利用者が多い空港の一つで、ホーチミン市の空の玄関口として機能しています。

ターミナル: 国際線と国内線に分かれており、近年は利用者増加に対応するため、**第三ターミナル（T3）**などの拡張・新設も進められています。

アクセス: ホーチミン市の中心部から比較的近く、移動に便利です。

ベトナム旅行やビジネスでホーチミン市を訪れる際に利用されることが多い空港です。

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Katie C
text:
Terrible experience.
My husband and I arrived at check-in and was stopped by an officer asking for our passport and trip confirmation number which I find odd because I never had anyone asking that in any countries that we've visited before.
Since I'm the only one flying (because I found out I was 8-weeks pregnant, so I changed my flight last minute to fly back home first while my husband stayed behind), the officer won't let my husband go with me to check-in to help me carry my heavy luggages.
After going back and forth, the officer said I'm lying because I don't look pregnant and start to yell at my husband that he cannot come with me.

So be aware of these "officers", they are rude and look down on all travelers.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Fox In The Snow
text:
Bali to HCM

Note yah tanpa tour and travel (solo traveler)

Turun dari pesawat jgn langsung ke imigrasi. Apalagi klo antrian imigrasi masih panjang. Imigrasi di HCM perlu minimal 1 -1,5 jam antrian.

Sebelum ke imigrasi beli dulu sim card dan tukar uang. Kalau kamu hanya HCM data rooming masih bisa, tapi jika kamu ingin ke dalat, Danang atau kota kota di luar HCM sebaiknya beli SIM Card Vietnam. Karna data roaming tidak bisa digunakan di luar HCM.

Setelah itu tukar uang (menurutku penukaran uang sblm imigrasi lebih murah). Lewat counter atau atm. Hampir sebagian besar transaksi di Vietnam masih didominasi dengan cash. Apalagi kalau kamu mau ke kota kota selain HCM, kabar buruknya atm (logo visa atau apapun) tidak berteman dengan mesin atm di sana 😭
Baru setelah itu antri imigrasi. Kemudian turun ke bawah ambil koper.

Kalau kamu menggunakan transportasi transfer dari bandara, biasanya mereka minta kamu tunggu di tiang berapa.tiang tiang di bandara HCM dibagian atasnya diberi nomor untuk memudahkan Driver bertemu dengan penumpang . Transportasi onlinenya bisa menggunakan uber.

Di luar bandara juga banyak resto dan mcd di bagian pojok sebelah kiri dari pintu keluar.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: M Dinh
text:
✈️ A Disappointing Experience at Tan Son Nhat Airport

Today, I traveled through Tan Son Nhat Airport with my baby, and I must say I was very disappointed with how some of the security staff behaved.

I completely understand that folding a baby stroller before the security scan is a regulation, and I fully respect that. However, the stroller I use cannot be folded, and in other airports I’ve been to, the officers simply assist by pushing the stroller through the standing scanner without any issue.

Instead of offering help or understanding, a few officers spoke in a very rude and unprofessional manner, which made the experience really unpleasant — especially when traveling with a baby.

I sincerely hope that Tan Son Nhat Airport management will review how their staff are trained and instructed to interact with passengers, so that travelers — especially parents with young children — can feel supported and respected.

A bit of courtesy and empathy can truly make a big difference in representing our country’s main international airport.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Hello me
text:
Ke Vietnam tanpa tour and travel.. sini kumpul pasukan solo traveler kayak aku.

Pas turun dari pesawat sebelum ke imigrasi yang perlu dilakukan adalah membeli sim card Vietnam. Siapkan paspor mu yah. Kenapa harus beli karna di beberapa daerah Vietnam data rooming tidak berfungsi total. Di HCM dan Hanoi masih aman tapi kalau kamu mau ke Da Lat, Da Nang, Hue, sapa.. pastikan beli sim card.

Setelah itu tukar uang. Di Vietnam hampir sebagian besar pakai cash. Kalau kamu hanya di HCM atau Hanoi aman untuk transaksi atm. Tapiiii… kalau di daerah dalat ntah kenapa visa dan Mastercard gagal. Ga berteman mereka sama mesin atm di sana 😓😭 Jadi jangan sampai kamu kehabisan cash kalau kamu ke area area selain kota besarnya.

Baru masuk ke imigrasi. Imigrasi Vietnam antriannya sangat panjang. Setelah lewat imigrasi baru turun ke lantai bawah untuk ambil koper. Kalau lapar diluar ada kayak kfc dan mcd. Makan dulu sebelum lanjut. Kalau kamu ga mau repot dengan bawa koper, bisa pesan transportasi lewat aplikasi Traveloka untuk transfer dari bandara ke tempat tujuan di HCM. Mereka biasanya akan menghubungi lewat wa dan nanti disuruh nunggu di tiang mana. Di bagian atas tiang tiang dikasih nomor.

Kemudian di Vietnam mau di kota atau daerahnya ada transportasi online : UBER. Jadi aman yah. Ga boncos untuk ongkos.

text:
I was on business trip and people who came to pick me up
had to wait for me because of Slow Immigration process

The immigration process was extremely hectic — we had to wait in line for over an hour. This level of delay is quite frustrating, especially after a long journey.

I would strongly urge the local authorities to look into improving the efficiency of immigration services. During our wait, we heard from locals that offering a few extra dollars placed in your passport to nearby officers can reportedly lead to a smoother, faster process. If true, this is deeply concerning and completely unacceptable.

Such practices undermine the integrity of the system and should be addressed seriously.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Hej
text: For one of the busiest airports in SEA, this airport is much below average. Getting through immigration is one thing (very manual process hence long, slow moving queues), when you eventually cross over, prepare to be underwhelmed. Facilities are old and lacks a lot of the basic things an airport should offer. There are limited power points, so that people are awkwardly standing next to the wall and for the power points that are not boarded up (came across a few), they are loose so you can't plug anything in. Ended up buying a cup of coffee purely to use the power point at the cafe. There is limited seating so lots of people standing, lack of signage to indicate water refill stations, and limited food options (all prices displayed in USD, VND is accepted). Hopefully the new airport planned for opening in 2026 is better designed.

text:
Worst experience ever
Firstly, due to traffic we missed the flight from ho chi Minh city to phuquoc. After paying an extra fee of around we got a rescheduled flight in the morning.
Secondly, the morning flight time is 9:20am and as per the check in rules we are inside the airport before 8:40 am but there's a huge line and we were late for 3 mins at check in counter. As a matter of grace they could've done something to let us in.
Instead they saw our rescheduled tickets and wasted our time saying we were late and counter is closed to check in. Initially we didn't understand what's going on. Later, after the flight is in air, we were still in airport, then they started explaining the rules that if a person is twice late, then his ticket will be cancelled. This is a fraud way to loot from people.
Sad part is you can't even get any proper support to speak on these kind of issues where the mistake is not from your end but from theirs.
Loopholes
You either leave your extra cabin weight aside without getting a tag for it,because once you check-in you should come back to get into security check, you cancollectit then. This can be the best way to skip the EXTRA weight.
Or if you have any extra baggage then they won't consider your cabin weight, however their main moto is to loot the people let them feel that they are looting us.
You won't even get water for free in flight better carry a bottle with you

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Vũ Hoàng
text:
👍:
1.	Kết nối mạnh, đa dạng đường bay quốc tế và nội địa
Sân bay Tân Sơn Nhất là cửa ngõ lớn nhất miền Nam Việt Nam, có mạng bay nội địa lẫn quốc tế đông đảo. Điều này giúp nhiều lựa chọn chuyến bay, hãng bay cho hành khách. Báo cáo “Megahubs 2025” của OAG cho biết Tân Sơn Nhất lần đầu lọt vào Top 50 sân bay có mạng bay quốc tế kết nối tốt nhất thế giới, ở vị trí 45.  ￼
2.	Vị trí thuận lợi
Gần trung tâm TP. Hồ Chí Minh, dễ dàng tiếp cận bằng taxi, xe buýt, các phương tiện công cộng. Nhiều người xem đây là lợi thế lớn so với các sân bay xa trung tâm.
3.	Quy mô lớn, khả năng phục vụ đông khách
Sân bay có lưu lượng hành khách rất cao, đặc biệt trong mùa cao điểm (Tết, lễ hội, dịp nghỉ dài). Việc này phần nào cho thấy sân bay có năng lực vận hành nhất định.  ￼
4.	Đã và đang có sự cải thiện, mở rộng
Có các dự án mở rộng, nâng cấp cơ sở hạ tầng, ví dụ như nhà ga T3, tăng công suất phục vụ để giảm áp lực cho toàn sân bay.  ￼

👎:
1.	Hiệu suất đúng giờ thấp
Theo bảng xếp hạng AirHelp năm 2025, sân bay Tân Sơn Nhất được cho điểm thấp về “đúng giờ” so với nhiều sân bay quốc tế.  ￼
Việc này ảnh hưởng rất rõ tới trải nghiệm hành khách, khiến nhiều chuyến bay bị muộn, chờ đợi lâu.
2.	Dịch vụ hành khách, tiện nghi & trải nghiệm chưa ổn định
•	Khách phản ánh về thủ tục, check-in, an ninh, xuất nhập cảnh có chỗ đông đúc, ùn tắc, thời gian chờ đợi lâu.  ￼
•	Vệ sinh, nhà vệ sinh, không gian chờ vẫn đôi khi bị phản ánh là chưa tốt ở các thời điểm cao điểm.  ￼
•	Các tiện ích mua sắm, ăn uống, wifi, giá cả có khi bị cho là chưa tương xứng với kỳ vọng hoặc đắt so với chất lượng.  ￼
3.	Quá tải trong các dịp cao điểm
Khi lưu lượng khách lên cao (Tết, lễ hội, mùa du lịch), sân bay thường rơi vào tình trạng đông đúc, hành khách phải chờ làm thủ tục từ sáng sớm, khu vực gửi xe, lối vào sân bay nhiều khi kẹt.  ￼
4.	Chưa đáp ứng kỳ vọng quốc tế về trải nghiệm hành khách
Sân bay bị xếp thấp trong các bảng đánh giá quốc tế về trải nghiệm hành khách (dịch vụ, ẩm thực, mua sắm, tiện nghi). Ví dụ Tân Sơn Nhất xếp 248/250 sân bay được khảo sát bởi AirHelp năm 2025 với tổng điểm thấp hơn nhiều sân bay trong khu vực.  ￼

Nói chung, Tân Sơn Nhất có vai trò rất quan trọng và có nhiều ưu điểm về mạng bay và vị trí. Nhưng để nâng cao trải nghiệm người dùng và cạnh tranh quốc tế, cần ưu tiên cải thiện:
1.	Quản lý thời gian chờ, đúng giờ: cải thiện phối hợp giữa các bộ phận thủ tục, an ninh, mặt đất; tăng khả năng xử lý trong giờ cao điểm.
2.	Mở rộng và nâng cấp cơ sở vật chất: nhà ga, phòng chờ, lối đi, chỗ ngồi chờ, thiết bị hỗ trợ hành khách (wifi, cổng sạc, biển chỉ dẫn rõ ràng hơn).
3.	Chất lượng vệ sinh, phục vụ: đảm bảo tiêu chuẩn sạch sẽ, nhất là nhà vệ sinh, phòng chờ, khu ăn uống; cải thiện thái độ và chất lượng phục vụ nhân viên.
4.	Giá cả & tiện ích ăn uống / mua sắm: đảm bảo đa dạng hơn, hợp lý hơn, đặc biệt ở các khu sau kiểm soát an ninh, cổng lên máy bay.
5.	Giao thông xung quanh sân bay: giảm ùn tắc, cải thiện việc ra vào sân bay, tăng phương tiện công cộng kết nối hiệu quả hơn.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Stefan Urban
text: Took me 5min to go through Munich immigration and security. Here it takes an 1hr+. Probably they work extra slow, since they are offering express lines if you pay $20/$30. So, they have an incentive to work slow. Essentially this is another form of corruption. Very common here in Vietnam. So, this airport is very outdated. Immigration is  very slow, unfriendly and not service oriented. Also the fact that there are a dozen queues, instead of having one or two and delegating people to the immigration counters shorty before it's your turn, doesn't help. It's pure luck if your queue is moving or not. Some of the officials, if not most of them, work rather slowly. That's one of the few things that are still very communist in this country. It would not take much to improve this, but I have little hope that we see any change soon. On the positive, the airport is relatively compact/small, and once you queued for an hour at immigration, distances are short. Although that also means that not all airplanes are accessible through gates.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Tarnya Chittenden
text:
If you're coming to this airport, I very much recommend getting a fast past. The line for immigration is more than off putting, especially if you have an invisible chronic illness that means standing in line for hours is something that you may struggle to do.

While the staff are polite, 40 minutes out from the boarding time on my return flight to Melbourne, they performed a water check. Causing everyone at the gate to go upstairs, line up and display their bags to show they weren't carrying water. If you did have water, you had to throw it in a bin. Now I had just purchased 2 bottles of water to drink before my flight, as it's high levels of humid and I am becoming rapidly dehydrated. I now get to wait at least an hour, before I get to spend yet more money on water. I am so angry. What an absolute waste. This should have been done when they check our tickets, or closer to board time.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Wankey Teo
text:
I had about 4.5 hours of layover in Ho Chi Minh (Tan Son Nhat International Airport). After reading reviews about the long immigration queues, I decided to book the fast-track service the night before my trip, since my transfer required me to collect my luggage and re-check in. I found the cheapest option on Agoda, and the service provider messaged me on WhatsApp within an hour of payment.

I still think it’s a bit strange for an airport to offer such a service, but when I saw how crowded immigration was, I was so glad I made the right call.

Grabbed something and chilled at a café before boarding — prices were reasonable. (Tip: skip BK!)

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Noah Qi
text: Terrible airport experience. Poor service, rude staff, and confusing directions. After landing, I asked an airport worker where to go for international transfer — he didn’t even look up from his phone and just pointed ahead saying ‘anywhere.’ After waiting 30 minutes in line, I was told it was the customs exit and had to go elsewhere — again, just a finger point, no words. Tried asking another staff member, same thing: silent finger pointing, impatience, and attitude when we sought clarification. One of the worst airport experiences I’ve ever had. AVOID THIS PLEACE if you can.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: English A1
text:
The experience I had during my departure from Tan Son Nhat International Airport (SGN) was nothing short of a disaster and, frankly, an insult. This review is written with a cold rage stemming from a level of disrespect I have rarely encountered.

1. Chaotic and Incompetent Queue Management:
The immigration process was a complete failure. It took a staggering two hours to clear customs. There was absolutely no effective control or guidance for the queues. Without any warning or instruction from the staff, the line I was standing in was suddenly and arbitrarily closed, forcing everyone to scramble and reform in other lanes. This created immediate chaos and significantly extended the wait time for hundreds of passengers.

2. The Most Disrespectful Official:
When I finally reached the immigration counter, I was met by a male officer, approximately in his 30s and wearing glasses, who was undeniably the worst gatekeeper a country could employ. He maintained a perpetually sullen and unprofessional demeanor, failing to utter a single word to me throughout the entire process. The climax of this disrespectful encounter was when he literally tossed my passport and boarding pass back to me.

This level of unprofessional and outright contemptuous behavior is simply unforgivable, especially at the main gateway to a country, where first and final impressions are formed. This was, without a doubt, one of the most disrespectful interactions I have ever had with an official.

I am still shaking with frustration as I write this. This airport urgently needs to address the systemic incompetence in its operations and immediately terminate or retrain staff members who treat international visitors with such appalling contempt.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Phùng Bá Đồng
text: Nhà ga hành khách quốc tế T3 – Sân bay Tân Sơn Nhất được thiết kế với kiến trúc hiện đại, sang trọng và mang phong cách mở, thể hiện sự kết hợp hài hòa giữa công năng, thẩm mỹ và yếu tố sinh thái. Không gian bên trong ga rộng lớn, thoáng đãng với trần cao uốn cong ấn tượng, hệ thống đèn LED được bố trí đều khắp tạo cảm giác lung linh và hiện đại. Sàn lát đá sáng màu, cùng những hàng ghế chờ màu đỏ nổi bật, tạo điểm nhấn cho khu vực trung tâm. Các khu chức năng như quầy làm thủ tục, khu kiểm tra an ninh, cửa ra tàu bay (Gate 01–13) và khu dịch vụ được bố trí khoa học, thuận tiện cho hành khách di chuyển. Dọc theo hai bên là hàng cây xanh và tường kính lớn kéo dài suốt chiều cao nhà ga, giúp tận dụng ánh sáng tự nhiên và mở rộng tầm nhìn ra khu bay. Tổng thể, T3 mang lại trải nghiệm không gian hiện đại, tinh tế và đẳng cấp quốc tế, góp phần nâng tầm hình ảnh của sân bay Tân Sơn Nhất nói riêng và Thành phố Hồ Chí Minh nói chung.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Zlatan Simonov
text: The airport is well fine at best in most things but the main problems are: the insanely long border control process when you arrive or depart internationally, because it is all done by a person, including the hot and humid Vietnamese weather. The second, is this abysmal international interchange process, you arrive, you get checked, seems fine and normal, and you take a bus to another terminal. Now, you are told absolutely nothing about where to go, and you have to find the signs on where to go. Then you are taken for an identical processes, of immigration, and luggage check, which you did before the bus, and makes absolute no sense. The immigration process for us, it was my family, my mum, dad me and younger brother. My mum, brother and I came out first to this lady, as we were going to Melbourne and my dad was going to frankfurt. We already observed the lady was slow and stupid. We came up to her, my mum puts our three passports on the table, she looks at my mum, no words, no nothing. My mum realises and puts her boarding pass. Same reaction. Finally she takes them. Points at me to move somewhere. I move where she points, then she does some other movement that I can't even describe, somewhat like a dismissal wave, and no words? Finally I understood and walk off behind the red line, for absolutely no reason. After a good while, she checked all four of us. She was slow, for genuinely no readon. If I'm ever going to have an interchange flight through Vietnam, definitely through Hanoi.

text:
Bad experience.  Too too much crowded, with not enough staff to handle anything, not sure how they will manage in season (this is rainy/typhoon season). there were huge queues for bag drop/boarding pass, immigration and security check - it took almost 2 hours to reach the gate, and we were completely exhausted.
the luggage storage place asks for 400000VND for 3 hours and only cash without the neighboring ATM's working (sunday out of cash). Domestic terminal has 4 ATM's , while international only 1.
If they cannot cater to so many flights, why not keep all desks staffed and increase space - there is no point of even kids and elderly spending hours in queues !!!

name: Yeoh Yuxian
text:
One of the worst airports I’ve been to. The immigration process is painfully slow, and it feels like nothing is organized properly. Even if your flight lands on time, you can easily get stuck at immigration for an hour or more.

What’s even crazier is that they actually sell “fast pass” options on Klook — like you’re buying a skip-the-line ticket for Universal Studios. It really says a lot about how bad the system is.

If you’re flying out of here, plan your timing carefully and give yourself plenty of buffer, especially for international departures. Definitely not the best way to end a trip.

text: Unbelievably long queues at the arrival and departure immigration. You have to take of everything at teh security as it's more like they are using 2000s airport equipments in a place where so many tourists come.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Dinesh Sanjivi
text:
Okay where do I start? We had a disappointing experience at the international airport. First of all why are the staffs so arrogant and unfriendly? Its fine to be strict but not arrogant. We are visiting your country as a tourist, and we expect for some warm, friendly welcome, not to be treated like we came there without passport. Next, is the language barrier most of them cant speak English, at least have some staffs that can speak english that can direct us where to go and where to line up.

The whole immigration check is so messy, without proper line and instructions on where to line up and where to go. Everyone seem to be clueless and start to ask each other before being sure where to line up.

And probably this is a rare airport that still does not have a digital check in and you got to bring physical printed boarding pass. Yes I know it was written in the boarding pass, but it is easily missed by most of us. So many of them had to wait the whole queue to find out this and had to go back to print the boarding pass at the counter and line up again to pass the immigration. Why cant you announce/put a board to avoid this problem, you're just causing more chaos.

Basically all the good experience that you had at the city is spoiled because of the hassle you had to go through in the airport.
Hope there will be improvement and some changes, if I were to go back there one day.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Diandra Family
text:
Tan Son Nhat International Airport is one of the worst airports I have ever experienced. The staff were not only unfriendly but also arrogant toward regular passengers, while showing an overly nice and respectful attitude to those using “fast track” services. The queues were painfully long, the toilets filthy, and the whole place felt chaotic and unprofessional.

What makes it even worse is the blatant unfairness: agents which provide fast track services, their clients are treated like VIPs, and shockingly, some are even allowed to use the diplomatic lane—something that should never happen. This creates the impression that unless you pay extra, you will be treated badly.

For an international gateway, this is disgraceful. The management must seriously improve their standards, because right now, Tan Son Nhat gives a terrible image of Vietnam to the world.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Yuya Fukasugi
text:
worst airport among not small my flight experience.
Yes, I have used several airports with worse equipment and services, but they had fewer passengers at the same time.

The real tragedy of this airport lies in the imbalance between the number of passengers and the quality of service. This results in unexpectedly long waiting times everywhere. Because of this, many people try to process their check-in far too early. However, the airport lacks any system to prioritize or organize passengers according to departure time, which creates major chaos.

Here, I must share my embarrassing mistakes: I missed my flights two days in a row because I could not reach the departure gate on time. Of course, it is my own fault, but I had never failed to catch a flight at any other airport with the same time sense. At this airport, however, it seems necessary to arrive at least three hours in advance, just as the overly cautious guidance recommends.

text: Do not store valuables in your luggage. My checked bag was opened and a red envelope containing lucky money from a wedding was stolen (the envelope was taped shut and the tape was ripped with the contents gone when i checked upon landing). The flight with Vietjet was fine, i had no issues with check in or take off. My flight was Vietjet so there was no free services, chargers or television sets. On that note there are virtually zero working wall chargers or charging stations in the airport so bring a battery bank. Also if you enter any store, you will be followed and watched like they expect you to steal things and it is very uncomfortable. Really soured the end of my visit that I otherwise enjoyed.

name: Robert Limanek
text:
On arrival, the line at immigration (for foreigners) was over 1 hour! Not acceptable! The bus 152 to the center (and Bui Vien) was good; pick-up just outside the front door to the international terminal (5,000 VND), a very short wait.
On departure, the line at Vietnam immigration was about 30 minutes (just to leave the country!). The security was about 10 minutes. The 2 water machines were broken - I was able to refill (with hot water) at a coffee shop. There are crowded, inconvenient shuttle buses to the airplanes (presumably all flights) - make sure to be there early (30 minutes seems enough).
If travelling to Phnom Penh, I advise taking a bus.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Anika
text:
🚨 Beware of Taxi Scams & Unhelpful Airport Security

I had a very unpleasant and unsafe experience when I landed here:
1. After arrival, my phone battery was low and I had no Wi-Fi. A man approached me and offered a taxi for 130,000 VND to my hotel. I agreed, but he led me to a different driver. That driver immediately asked me to pay 110,000 VND upfront just for “airport fees” before we even started driving (!) even we had agreed on 130,000 total. I refused and got out of the car. Later I found out the price is only about 70,000 VND + 10,000 VND airport fee.

2. Because my phone was about to die, I tried to go back into the terminal to use Wi-Fi or charge it so I could order a safe ride. Security refused to let me back in, even after I politely explained the situation. They told me they didn’t care and sent me away.

This felt unsafe and extremely unwelcoming.

Advice for other travelers:
Download the Grab app before you land and book directly through it.
Don’t follow anyone offering rides outside . most are scams or overpriced.

Feedback for airport management:
Denying access to Wi-Fi and help when someone’s phone is dying is not acceptable. This attitude discourages tourism and creates safety risks for travelers.

name: HL Y.
text:
it was a pretty confusing experience transiting between domestic and international flights here. from the international to domestic terminals, one had to take the bus after the baggage claim.

but from domestic to international, one has to go through security at the new domestic terminal after baggage claim, take a bus to the old domestic terminal, and then walk to the International terminal for security again.

it could be way less confusing for travellers if the bus directly takes one from the new domestic terminal to the international one without having to undergo security checks first.

The priority pass/loungekey lounge at the new domestic terminal is great, but the two in the international terminal are quite lacklustre. the food is cold and not refilled often and for a country with such excellent food, these have to be my worst meals in Vietnam. skip them if you can

name: Puah Sinling
text:
I had a terrible experience at this airport. Scammers pretended to be Grab drivers. They approached me, acted as if they were helping me check the car plate, and then quickly showed me a fake screenshot to make it look like they were my driver. As a result, I got into the wrong car.

After realizing I didn’t have cash, they told me to change to another car. This was clearly a scam designed to trick travelers, and it made me feel unsafe right after arriving.

Airports should pay more attention to these scams and take strict action against fake drivers operating here.

Travelers, please be very careful — only use the official Grab app, verify the car plate directly in the app, and do not trust anyone approaching you outside the app.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Blake Griffin
text:
The airport isn’t ridiculously big, which is nice. It’s shaped like a long rectangle, so the check-in counters and departure gates are basically spread out in a straight line. Pretty easy to navigate without getting lost.

Food options are decent — there are a few places to grab a bite just outside the departure area where cars drop you off, and more food spots and gift shops inside near the check-in counters. Seating is a bit limited before security, so don’t expect to find plenty of waiting space. After security and immigration, you’ll find smaller souvenir shops, a couple of food courts, and the Apricot Lounge (you can buy access). That lounge is especially handy since there’s no Qantas lounge in Saigon.

Arrival is super straightforward. There’s just one immigration entrance right in the middle of the building, and from there you head downstairs to baggage claim and then through customs on the same floor. Smooth process overall.

Dann steht man 35min an der immigration an (5 schalter offen, für alle). Dann steht man und halten sie sich fest nochmal 45min. An, an einem einzigen schalter, also alle Abflüge an einem offenen schalter wo eine einzige person die boardingpässe aller personen scannt.  Danach wartet man erneut 30min am security check wo ganze 4 scanner alle abflüge abfertigen.
Ich war 4h vorher am flughafen, alles unter 2,5 eher 3 Stunden vorher und man verpasst safe seinen Abflug.
Wahnsinn.
Zu beachten ist auch, dass terminal 3, 2, 1 je woanders sind, sprich mit dwm shuttelbus wenn man wechseln muss auch 30min. Einplanen sollte.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Daniel Yoon
text:
Vietnam’s busiest airport is one of the weakest I’ve experienced—stifling heat, poor facilities, chaotic boarding, and unreliable airline service. A functional gateway, but hardly a welcoming one.

Full Review:
Tan Son Nhat International Airport (Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất), Vietnam’s largest airport serving Ho Chi Minh City, is unfortunately one of the weakest international travel experiences I’ve encountered. And that is saying something.

I always arrive at new places hoping to be pleasantly surprised—in this case, perhaps by unexpected design flourishes, cultural nods, or even just thoughtful conveniences. But here, that sense of discovery was nowhere to be found. Instead, Tan Son Nhat feels like a bare-bones facility meant only to process travelers, not welcome them.

To be fair, it is not right to compare Vietnam’s hub to the crown jewels of Asia—Singapore’s Changi, Tokyo’s Haneda, or Seoul’s Incheon. But even when weighed against more modest airports in Malaysia, Thailand, or Indonesia, Tan Son Nhat falls short.

Inside, the environment is uncomfortable: air conditioning is nearly non-existent, leaving the air heavy and stagnant. The Wi-Fi, when it works at all, is frustratingly unreliable. The retail mix is peculiar—some luxury boutiques exist, but most are cheap knock-off stores and eateries. Boarding is another ordeal, with passengers herded onto buses and ferried to their planes rather than boarding directly from gates.

The issues extended to the national airline as well. Our Vietnam Airlines flight had a crew that arrived late, no announcements or updates to explain the delay, and an eventual departure that was far later still. That lack of communication is simply inexcusable in modern travel.

All in all, Tan Son Nhat left me with the impression of an airport stuck in a different era. It is Vietnam’s busiest air gateway, but it does not reflect the vibrant, rapidly modernizing nation it represents. With meaningful investment, it could become a proud hub for the country. Until then, it remains a place of disappointment and missed opportunities.

Verdict: 1/5 — a functional gateway, but a dismal travel experience that desperately needs modernization and better management.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: caca lorensha
text:
I had a very disappointing experience at Tân Sơn Nhất Airport while transiting after a short 2-day stay in Vietnam. On 22 September 2025, I was flying from Ho Chi Minh City to Jakarta via Singapore, and airport security forced me to check in a sealed 1-liter bottle of alcohol that I had legally purchased at Istanbul Airport Duty Free.
The bottle was inside a sealed, tamper-proof duty-free bag with the receipt visible, fully in line with international travel regulations. I’ve traveled internationally many times, and I’m fully aware of the rules. This item was completely compliant — under the 1-liter limit, purchased before my arrival in Vietnam, and never opened.
However, security staff insisted I could not bring it onboard, without giving any valid explanation. I was told to check it into my luggage, which made no sense since I was already through check-in and had very little time left before boarding. I had to go through long immigration lines again and was at risk of missing my flight.

This has never happened to me in any other airport. I had even reviewed the airport's own published rules, which clearly allow sealed duty-free alcohol in hand luggage under specific conditions.
I hope the airport authorities take this seriously. Clear communication, consistent enforcement of rules, and better staff training are urgently needed. No traveler should have to go through this kind of unnecessary trouble, especially when following the rules.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Ryuichi Kozaki
text:
ホーチミン市街中心部から北西約8kmの位置にある。IATAコードは「SGN」であり、ホーチミン市の旧名サイゴンが由来である。

T1（国内線）、T2（国際線）、T3←2025年4月開業（国内線）として運用している。

市街地とのアクセスは良いが、イミグレの慢性的な混雑（1時間超）＆暑さがしんどい。割り込みも多いから気を強く持つ事。

有料（事前予約）で入国審査や税関手続きを優先的に行うことができるファストトラックサービスがある。

Trip.com・kkday・Agoda等で予約・購入可能。価格（2〜3,000円/人）は時期と予約サイトによって変動している。

イミグレ付近で氏名を記載したプラカードを持ったスタッフが待機→キャビンクルーの隣のレーンに案内→2〜30分で通過。

出費は嵩むが、フライト後の体力温存に深夜便利用時は価値がある。

市内中心部より東方約40kmの位置に建設中の「ロンタイン国際空港」が2026年に開港予定。

Có thể nói T3 không chỉ là một nhà ga hàng không, mà còn là một điểm nhấn kiến trúc – quá đẹp, quá rộng, mang đến trải nghiệm đẳng cấp ngay từ những bước chân đầu tiên.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Lam Quang Tuan
text:
Sân bay quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất là sân bay lớn nhất Việt Nam về diện tích và công suất phục vụ hành khách. Nằm ở quận Tân Bình, TP. Hồ Chí Minh, cách trung tâm thành phố khoảng 8km, sân bay đóng vai trò là cửa ngõ giao thương chính của TP.HCM và khu vực miền Nam, với kết nối đa dạng các chuyến bay nội địa lẫn quốc tế, đặc biệt hướng đến khu vực châu Á - Thái Bình Dương .

Sân bay có tổng diện tích hơn 1,500 ha với 2 đường băng dài hơn 3,000m, 15 đường lăn và hơn 80 vị trí đỗ máy bay, đủ khả năng tiếp nhận nhiều loại máy bay lớn như Airbus A380, Boeing 747... Về nhà ga, sân bay có ba nhà ga chính gồm:

Nhà ga T1 nội địa (khoảng 40.948 m²), phục vụ các chuyến bay nội địa với 2 tầng thiết kế khu vực làm thủ tục, kiểm soát an ninh và phòng chờ hành khách.

Nhà ga T2 quốc tế (khoảng 115.834 m²) gồm 4 tầng, phục vụ các chuyến bay quốc tế, với khu vực nhập cảnh, xuất cảnh, hải quan và các tiện ích đi theo.

Nhà ga T3 nội địa mới được mở vào năm 2025, với diện tích 112.500 m², trang bị hiện đại, phục vụ chính cho Vietnam Airlines và Vietjet Air nhằm giảm tải cho nhà ga T1 .

Hàng năm, sân bay phục vụ trên 40 triệu lượt khách, là trung tâm vận chuyển hàng không lớn nhất cả nước về tần suất khai thác. Sân bay cũng cung cấp nhiều dịch vụ tiện ích như cửa hàng miễn thuế, dịch vụ y tế, wifi miễn phí, dịch vụ phòng khách hạng thương gia và nhiều dịch vụ hỗ trợ hành khách khác

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Binh Nguyen
text:
Tôi về tới sân bay vào khoảng 1h30 sáng ngày 24/06/2025 từ chuyến bay Trung Quốc – Việt Nam, và phải nói là quá thất vọng với cách tổ chức nhập cảnh ở đây.

Khu vực nhập cảnh quá tải trầm trọng, công dân Việt Nam và người nước ngoài xếp chung hàng dài tới 6–7 hàng, chen lấn, nóng nực. Không hề có bất kỳ biển báo hay nhân viên nào chủ động hướng dẫn người Việt đi theo lối riêng hoặc sử dụng máy tự động nhập cảnh. Chỉ ai tự đi hỏi thì được chỉ, mà cũng bị chỉ một cách lơ là, thiếu trách nhiệm.

Tệ hơn nữa, tôi tận mắt thấy có một số nhóm người nước ngoài và người Việt được “tự ý” tách hàng riêng, trong khi những người khác thì vẫn phải xếp hàng dài mệt mỏi. Khi tôi phát hiện có hàng riêng và đi sang, góp ý nhẹ nhàng với nhân viên xuất nhập cảnh, thì lại bị tỏ thái độ khó chịu. Người này còn nói kiểu dằn mặt, đe dọa sẽ không cho tôi nhập cảnh, bắt quay về xếp lại hàng với khách quốc tế.

Tôi thật sự không thể tin được đây là cách công dân Việt Nam bị đối xử khi trở về chính quê hương mình. Trong khi đó, nhiều khách quốc tế xung quanh thì lắc đầu ngao ngán, than phiền như thể đang xếp hàng trong một cái chợ. Điều này làm xấu đi rất nhiều hình ảnh đất nước trong mắt bạn bè quốc tế.

Tôi không yêu cầu đặc quyền gì, chỉ cần sự tôn trọng tối thiểu và hướng dẫn rõ ràng. Vào mùa du lịch mà công tác phân luồng, quản lý quá kém, lại còn thiếu chuyên nghiệp và thái độ tệ như vậy – thật sự là điểm trừ lớn cho sân bay này

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Nandana
text:
WORST EXPERIENCE EVER. I would give zero star if there was an option. Queue here is endless at ticketing and immigrations. There was no web check in option at Vietjet and staff are working at slowest pace possible without regard to people who are waiting. Half of the counters were empty and there is no ground staff to help and even if you find one, they pretend not to understand or speak English.

Staff behavior is very rude and unreasonable and when i reached counter I was asked to go back in the immigration queue without explanation where I had already waited for 1 hr 20 mins and had to get help from others in the queue to make it past immigration. Be there 5 hrs before if you want to make the flight.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: NV 5
text:
Wenn ich könnte würde ich null Sterne geben!
Meiden Sie diesen Flughafen wie der Teufel das Weihwasser. Man wartet 2 Stunden an einer völlig sinnlosen Passkontrolle. Obwohl mindestens doppelt so viele Schalter zu besetzen werden, spart das inkompete Flughafenmanagement offensichtlich an Personal, und dass in einem derartigen Niedriglohnland. Die Passkontrolle dient einzig und allein der Kontrolle des Einreisedatums, das könnte man genauso gut digital lösen. Nachdem man die ewige Passkontrolle überstanden hat, trifft man auf die geballte Inkompetenz der Security Kontrolle. Hier wird Stundenschieberei groß geschrieben. Ich rate ihnen nie von diesem Flughafen abzufliegen, eine Schande für Vietnam

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Anthony Boyle
text:
Checked in at 10:30am Monday morning. From there we had a 45 minute queue at the Immigration and hand baggage inspection. Very, very slow (or just a busy time?).

Once past the security checks it’s just one long airport terminal with the same shops at either end (maybe they’re different, but they looked the same).

The Huge Shock to me was how expensive everything is there. Coffee, food, snacks - all Way too expensive compared to the city. And yes, I know that airports are expensive, but this was over the top.

Before our flight I bought a bottle of water (500ml Aquafina brand). This cost me VN40,000 !!!  For comparison, the same brand of water costs just VN20,000 for TWO LITRES downtown in any of the Circle K shops.  This wasn’t overcharging, it’s extortion !!

(Wise travellers note that there are free water-filling stations in the airport. Bring your empty bottle).

Nhà ga T3 là nhà ga mới, views đẹp tuyệt đối điện ảnh
Nhà ga có 4 tầng, tầng trên cùng là các phòng chờ

Khi đi nhà ga T3 chú ý là cách khá xa nhà ga cũ nha, nên đi sớm hơn 20p so với kế hoạch cũ để kịp thay đổi. Tốt nhất khi bay bạn đến sân bay sớm hơn 2 tiếng so với giờ bay để kịp phòng những trường hợp rủi ro .

Bạn có thể đặt các loại xe công nghệ và liên lạc những nhân viên tại sân bay để được hỗ trợ

___

T3 station is a new station, absolutely beautiful views of cinema

The station has 4 floors, the top floor is the waiting rooms

When going to T3 terminal, pay attention that it is quite far from the old station, so you should go 20 minutes earlier than the old plan to keep up with the change. It is best to arrive at the airport 2 hours earlier than the flight time to keep up with risky cases.

You can book technology vehicles and contact airport staff for support

name: Hương Trần
text: Nhân viên cửa an ninh rất thái độ với hàng khách, hôm nay tôi qua cửa an ninh với 2 khay đồ, 1 khay bị quét lại, tôi đi ra bỏ quét lại, khi vào tôi đi chen vào vì nghĩ đã đi qua cửa quét người r không cần kiểm tra lại để lấy đồ ở khay còn lại vì có đồ giá trị, xong tôi bị nhân viên an ninh nhấn đầu trở lại cửa với thái độ rất coi thường. Tôi có mang kính và anh ta nhấn ngay mặt tôi không nể nang gì, không sợ làm hàng khách bị thương chỉ để cản tôi. Tôi có giải thích tại sao tôi làm vậy thì thay vì nhắc nhở tôi thì nhân viên đó lại không thèm nói gì chỉ lắc đầu chắp hít, chẹp mỏ kêu:" không còn gì để nói" như thể tôi là người vô ý thức. Tôi vừa bị đau vì bị cấn mắt kính do lần nhấn mặt đó vừa bị coi như mấy mẹ vô ý thức😡. Nghĩa là bây giờ khách hàng có lỡ không làm đúng quy định của sân bay thì nhân viên sân bay không cần nhắc nhở mà thẳng tay đụng chạm rồi phán xét hàng khách như cha mẹ vậy hả. Hàng khách cũng tốn tiền để đi máy bay chứ không đi chùa!!!! Vệ sinh nơi công cộng kém sạch sẽ, thái độ nhân viên lòi lõm !!!!

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Karina INC
text:
“Traveling through Ho Chi Minh City Airport (T2) was much smoother than I expected! Many people online said you need to hire ‘helpers’ to get through customs and to your gate, but honestly that’s just business promotion. You do not need it. Everyone at the airport was helpful and professional.

✅ My advice: give yourself at least 3 hours before your flight — that’s more than enough for check-in, baggage wrapping, customs, and security.
✅ Access is easy, clear signs in English, and staff are ready to assist if you ask.
✅ Wrapping service is available right in front of T2 for luggage, very convenient.
✅ Customs lines moved fast and were well organized.

Overall, smooth experience at T2 and no stress at all. Just plan your time and you’ll be fine!”

Just so you know this is flight using qatar airways to doha to miami.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Mk Km
text:
Bonjour,le pire transfert,l'avion d'air france avait une heure et demi de retard,ils nous restaient encore deux heures.
Certes ont c'est un peu égaré, mais en passant les contrôles il y'avait beaucoup de monde,j'ai demandé à trois agents de nous faire passer en leur expliquant que l'avion aller partir dans 40 minutes aucune aide de leur part,un des policier au frontières m'a même poussé de la main pour reprendre la file.
Ont nous à pas facilité pour passer c'est ça qui m'a agacé.
Aucune personne pour vous aider et vous guider,la première fois que je vois un avion partir à l'heure exacte car nous avons pu arrivé à la porte d'embarquement à 10h25 l'heure du départ,ils nous ont fait faire demi tour,ils ont déchargé les bagages de l'avion que nous avons récupérer,ce temps ou ils ont immobilisé l'avion ont n'aurais pu embarqué. Nous avons pris d'autres billets pour    260€ chacun et nous attendons pour prendre l'avion celui du lendemain car 2 avions par jours.
Nous avons passé la journée et la nuit à l'aéroport.
Faite très attention quand vous venez ici de ne pas faire d'erreur,ce qui peut arrivé à n'importe qui et n'importe où mais se que veut chaque un d'entre nous c'est d'être écouté.
Merci à la dame pour les billets d'avion,merci à la dame de m'avoir rajouté un oeuf et du riz au restaurant
Globalement très mauvaise expérience.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất

Hư 1,2 cái thì không nói, đi kiếm để có được 1 bình lấy nước uống mà ko có nỗi 1 cái bình nào hoạt động. Vậy sao không dẹp luôn đi, để chi vậy??? Làm cảnh, lách luật? Hay để trưng cho có vậy? Thật sự rất tệ, ép khách phai đi mua, mà mua rẻ như sân bay nước ngoài như thái lan đi ko nói. Mua chai nào cũng phải 2$ trở lên, mà $ có rẻ đâu nó lên tận 26 ngàn hơn mà. Quá tệ
