title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Hùng Nguyễn
text:
I don't understand how it still gets 3 stars. This is the worst airport I know. I'm comparing it to Thailand and other countries that have recently installed AutoGates. I believe the machines here are extremely outdated. The machines for Vietnamese citizens are operated by foreign staff, not Vietnamese.

The instructions are unclear, and the machines fail to recognize people multiple times. It took me 2–3 tries, and on average, others faced the same issue. In many cases, people had to scan up to 10 times and still couldn't pass through the AutoGate.

The immigration process is excessively slow, and even foreign visitors get yelled at.

The officers here don’t speak English. It's disappointing that taxpayers fund their salaries. They must be happy with such an easy job.

Tôi không hiểu sao vẫn được 3*. Đây là sân bay tệ nhất tôi biết, tôi so sánh với Thái Lan,  và các nước mới lắp máy Autogate. Tôi tin rằng máy ở đâu là thế hệ cực cũ, máy cho người Việt mà người hướng dẫn là người nước ngoài không phải Việt nam. Hướng dẫn không rõ ràng ,máy không nhận diện rất nhiều lần, tôi mất 2 3 lần, trung bình người khác cũng vậy. Nhiều trường hợp quét tận 10 lần vẫn không qua được cổng Auto Gate.

Quy trình nhập và xuất cảnh quá lâu,  có nạt nộ cả người nước ngoài.

Và các sĩ quan ở đây không biết nói tiếng anh. Thật thất vọng vì người dân trả thuế để nuôi họ. Họ thật hạnh phúc khi có công việc nhàn hạ như vậy.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Sagar Saini
text:
I recently traveled through Tan Son Nhat International Airport (Ho Chi Minh City) with my pregnant wife and child, and unfortunately, our experience was extremely disappointing.

1. Security Check Issues for Pregnant Wife – My wife is pregnant, and our doctor strictly advised her not to pass through an X-ray scanner. At most international airports, alternative screening options are available for pregnant women. However, at this airport, the security staff did not understand English, and even after using a translation app to explain the situation, they completely ignored our request and forced her to pass through the scanner. Their rude and unprofessional behavior made the situation even worse.

2. Vietnam Airlines Check-in Issues – The staff at the airline’s counter were unhelpful regarding seat selection and baggage handling. Despite pre-booking our seats online, they assigned different seats and were unwilling to assist in resolving the issue.

3. Poor Hygiene in Washrooms – The airport washrooms were dirty, unhygienic, and out of tissues. For an international airport, this is highly unacceptable.

4. Lack of Baby Facilities – There were no proper diaper-changing areas for babies. This made it extremely inconvenient for us while traveling with a child.

Overall, this was one of the worst airport experiences I have had. The lack of basic facilities, poor customer service, and unprofessional behavior of the staff made our journey unnecessarily stressful. I hope the authorities take these issues seriously and improve the airport’s services.

One of the worst airport i have never been.

Everything is expensive inside, like almost 5 times the price of what you can find in the city.
And last but not least, after the security check i bought a bottle of water for my flight (4 dollar so more than 100k dong when it usually costs 20k dong) but when i went to my gate. There were two police officers that were checking all the bag looking for water and they made me threw my bottle that i legit bought 2 min ago in the shop next to my gate.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: ccmangb
text: Avoid like the plague.  The bus took 15 minutes to pick us up from the tarmac.  Immigration was a nightmare... the lines were ridiculous and it took more than an hour.  They obviously had the capacity to process 3x what they did, they simply didn't man the posts.  What's even stranger is that they didn't have any expedited routes for their own nationals so Vietnamese had to wait just as long n line with everyone else.  This is repeated upon departure, with the same long lines, and even worse lines at security.  Absolutely disgusting.  I've never had such a terrible experience in all the decades I've been flying.  They're building a new terminal, but if they don't actually provide the personnel to handle the processing, then it won't solve anything.  The current terminal has more than enough capacity, they simply don't man the stations.  Anybody going through this airport should budget at LEAST 1 hour for passport and security, and this is for the non peak times.

text:
The airport is very busy and chaotic. It is not always clear where to go for baggage claim and there are many people moving back and forth. After collecting the baggage, it's quite difficult to navigate between the crowd.

After exiting the terminal, unfortunately the chaos is even worse. It is very difficult to arrange a pick as there is not a very organized parking area to meet a driver. Instead, there are several "corridors" with constant traffic where you arrange to meet.  Meanwhile, there are people that try to cross the aforementioned corridors with all their luggage.

Overall, the experience was a bit overwhelming, especially in contrast to other airports such as in Hanoi and Da Nang. However, don't worry, just be a bit patient and everything will be alright.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Elaine Lin
text:
2025/3/5 (wed) departure review

📍13:30 arrived at airport (already find the queue full of luggages and boxes from other people)
📍14:40 airline counter opens
📍15:14 finally our turn at the counter (only had 3 groups of ppl in front)
📍15:25 finish boarding pass check in at the counter
(Check in only opens three hours before departure)
📍15:25 start immigration line
📍15:40 pass immigration
📍15:55 pass hand carry xray
📍17:05 intercom notification of sudden gate change
📍17:10 flight boarding time starts
📍17:40 gate closes
📍17:50 flight takeoff

Airport wasn’t very clean, the chairs are full of crumbs and food.
Burger King in the international terminal was ok, located on the second floor where there’s also a phuc long and highland coffee, great for last minute snack.
There’s also a massage place that has express 20min massage ranging from 20 USD.
Burger King also has a kiosk at gate 21 (i think) that you can order on the spot and the staff will bring you the food within 10 mins, so that’s pretty awesome ,just don’t know if it works as we walked to Burger King ourselves.

I was very lucky that arrival and departure wasn’t 3 hours like what other people have experienced, but I think it’s also because I deliberately chose a weekday to fly in and out. Maybe weekend will be more hectic.

We also did our online check in before, but somehow they won’t let us have the boarding pass QR code beforehand (we could do it in Taiwan, but not here in Ho Chi Minh),we had to line up for the boarding pass like everyone else, no special like for online check in 🤔

I think out of all the airports I’ve been to, this one stressed me out the most with the long queues 😩

Oh and one of our luggage handle broke, and it has been with us for 10 years, RIP, dunno if it was just the time to retire the bag, or the airport handling here is too rough 🤔

name: YF Sung
text:
Both arrival and departure at Tan Son Nhat Airport in Ho Chi Minh City are plagued by long queues. This time, I spent more than 1.5 hours just waiting to clear immigration upon arrival. There were no staff members guiding passengers, and which line led to a faster immigration officer seemed purely a matter of luck. Some travelers who queued at the same time as me but in a different line had already cleared immigration, while I still had at least ten more people ahead of me.
Due to the prolonged immigration process, by the time I reached baggage claim, the information screen no longer displayed the carousel number for my flight. I had to search for my luggage on my own.
There are widespread reports that paying extra can get you through immigration faster, and from what I observed, this seems to be true. While waiting in line, I noticed several staff members personally escorting travelers through a separate process, allowing them to clear immigration at a surprisingly fast pace. This raises the question: does this mean that all travelers are expected to pay extra if they want to save time?

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Eric Nguyen
text: Thật sự hôm nay 2/3/2025 rất bất súc về thái độ của bộ phận an ninh soi chiếu. Vợ mình đang có thai nên xét theo mức độ và quy định mới kèm theo hướng dẫn tại quầy kiếm tra an ninh được ưu tiên. Nên mình mới đi vào lối dành ưu tiên cho người có thai THEO HƯỚNG DẪN nhưng khi tới quầy có 1 đồng chí mặt tròn đeo mắt kính tóc ngắn nhìn từ xa thấy cũng hiền, bước tới hỏi thì lại nhận được 1 thái độ rất là khác so với khuôn mặt. Hỏi lần thứ 1 xin cho hỏi lối này dành cho phụ nữ có thai phải không thì nhận câu trả lời là không. Mình mới nói là BẢN HƯỚNG DẪN CÓ ĐỂ LỐI ĐI CHO PHỤ NỮ CÓ THAI, TRẺ EM VÀ XE LĂN. Đồng chí đó mới nhìn vợ mình ( bụng cũng to rồi được 27, tuần), xong trừng mắt với mình trả lời lại Không rất chi là cọc lóc với tỏ ra 1 thái độ hách dịch và hóng hánh, trịch thượng ( úi trời mình nghi đó là thái độ của người cán bộ với người dân hay sao ) rồi đuổi mình và vợ mang bầu xếp 1 hàng dài. Tuổi của đồng chí đó nhỏ hơn mình rất nhiều nhưng cách cư xử rất chi là thiếu văn minh. Mình là công dân việt nam còn bị như vậy thì người nước ngoài trong trường hợp mình họ sẽ nghĩ sao về đất nước mình. Thật sự đây là 1 điều rất đáng buồn. Để rõ ràng nên trong khi soi chiếu mình có hỏi những đồng chí khác xác nhận có thai được đi cổng ưu tiên hay không điều đáp lại là có. Mình lên mạnh tìm hiểu thì thấy thông tin cũng được ưu tiên. Thông tin có rất rõ ràng và kèm theo bảng hướng dẫn tại cổng kiểm tra an ninh rõ ràng nhưng cán bộ lại có thái độ với người dân như vậy thì tôi thật sự rất bất súc

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Sumita Shah
text:
While the airport is good, clean and staff are nice, beware of the sim card scamming lady on the stall as you come out of baggage claim into the arrivals hall. She is immediately on the left outside the doors.

She fraudulently sold me a mobifone package telling me it was 180gb 30 days and would allow top up.. cost 350000 vnd. I asked for Viettel but she said Viettel does not do top up.

Later, when i was in taxi, i got a text saying my sim card was only valid till 21 March.

She put the sim card in very quickly. And did not give me the actual card and details until i had paid. I downloaded the mobifone app to register the sim so i couldtop up later when 30 days were expiring.

I could not seem to register the sim so i went to the mobifone shop who confirmed i had been scammed. It was not a mobifone sim, it was local.vn. and it was not valid for 30 days. The mobifone rep helped me download the local.vn app. But when i registered the sim, it was listed to a Nguyen Van Benh, passport id ending 951. Dob 1.1.63.

I will take this up with my credit card company, but i want others to be aware of the sim card scammers at HoChi mihn airport. Fraudulently selling sims purporting to be mobifone.

name: Radek Zygulski
text:
Plusy:
Blisko centrum miasta i tani jest dojazd.

Minusy:
Zaczynając od toalet, brudno, ciasno, nie ma na tyle ubikacji i wszędzie się czeka. Toalety bez mydła pomimo tego że Pani sprzątała nie raz.

Przechowalnia walizek moim zdaniem jest dość droga. Cena jest 27,000 VND za godzinę lub 270,000VND za 10h lub więcej.
Bardzo małe pomieszczenie i czekaliśmy długo żeby odebrać walizki pomimo tego że stały tam one tylko 5h.

Na paszporty trzeba czekać długo w długich kolejkach.
Na przejście przez ochronę tak samo. Na tylu pasażerów parę bramek to jest masakra.
Byliśmy 3h przed czasem i było na styk.

grabのジャケットを着たスタッフさんに画面を見せると探してくれます。

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Alan Phua
text:
Unpredictable queuing times at the international terminal.

For regular outbound travellers, reaching 2 hours before flight is not just required, it is mandatory due to unpredictable customs clearance that can take anywhere from as fast as 15mins to more than an hour for outbound travellers.

Security clearance also requires at least a 15mins or more buffer.

Traffic to the airport during peak times can also be very unpredictable.

For inbound travellers arriving at TSN, the same unpredictability applies for customs clearance.

Airport is working hard to cope, so kudos to airport staff for doing their best everyday.

name: Bernd Schmid
text:
Steinzeitflughafen. Ich fühle mich ins Thailand der 90er Jahre zurückversetzt.
40 Minuten (!!) vor den Ausreiseschaltern angestanden, gefolgt von weiteren 25 Minuten an der Sicherheitskontrolle. Und damit es dort nicht zu schnell geht, wird jeder Reisepass nebst Boarding Pass noch einmal angeschaut. Dafür gibt es exakt einen Schalter mit einer Person. Ansonsten wäre es an der Sicherheitskontrolle sicher schneller gegangen. Wofür das? Am Ausreiseschalter wurden sowohl Reisepass als auch Boardingpässe genaustens angeschaut.
Soweit mir bekannt, ist an Wochenenden in Südostasien mit besonders vielen Flugreisenden zu rechnen. Nur scheint das in HCMC niemanden zu interessieren.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất

text:
This is the worst airport in South East asia. Doubting if they actually understand what aircraft is. Same for the Vietnamese airlines.  If you can avoid this airport do so ! Immigration takes so much time if you have a connecting flight from international to domestic flight you can be sure to miss the connection. Even domestic connections are risky because the airlines are slowed by the bad management of the airport.

Also I received the boarding pass of another passenger and noticed only after immigration but I had no time to change it so I  went trough security with and they didn't mentioned anything until I went in the bus after passing the gate, they scanned the boarding pass of the other passenger and finally noticed their mistake. I needed to get back inside and they printed the my boarding pass. Insane when for one error in your name or when you misplaced the order of your names some companies  force you to pay a fee to correct your name. So it was for me unbelievable to go trough security not even with my boarding pass.

Good luck if you go to this airport

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Happy Coach Yuantai
text:
The worst airport service I have experienced in my years of travel.
On 21 Feb, I was booked on a flight from Saigon to Singapore via Scoot TR303.
I arrived at the airport around 4pm and have already done check in online.
After queuing at the immigration lines for 45mins and reaching the front of the q, I was dismayed when the customs officer nonchalantly waved me away as I didn't have a printed ticket.
What???
This is the first time I see such a policy, making it compulsory for a printed ticket.
In this day and age?
Ok, I can't argue there. So I rushed back to the scoot counter and have them print out a ticket for me.
I was so fortunate my buddies are in queue and I don't have to requeue again. Requeue would have caused me to miss my flight.
That's not the end of ordeal.
After finally clearing customs (1 hour 15mins), my friends and I headed to the scheduled departure gate 17.
It was packed and we managed to find ourselves some seats and settled down to await boarding time.
At around 555pm (20 minutes before departure), one of my friends suddenly remarked ,"Oh no, I think they changed the departure gate to 12!"
Cue pandamonium and another mad scramble to the correct boarding gate.
We made it.
Just.
Overall, a pathetic experience and surely one of my worst.
I can craft a training programme, and am open to discussions.

text:
Extremadamente rudimentarios todos los procesos.
Llegamos en un vuelo doméstico desde Hanoi con conexión a Pukhet (Tailandia). Aterrizamos 7.30 hs; a pesar de volar con la misma compañía (VietJet) hay que retirar el equipaje y volver a hacer check-in.
El proceso de cambiar de terminal (caminata de 4 minutos)  hacer check-in, migraciones y control policial nos llevó 3.45 hs en el mismo aeropuerto! Casi perdemos el vuelo a Phuket.
Hay que hacer colas interminables, no tienen buenos modales,  los locales se colan y empujan…
Experiencia olvidable! Nos había sucedido algo similar al llgar.
Mi recomendación es evitar Ho Chi Min si pueden!

text:
市内まで車で40分ほど。渋滞しているともっとかかることがあるようです。
出国の際は3時間以上前に行くことを強くお勧めします。私は出国時、空港には2時間前に行きましたが、搭乗口に着いたのは出発５分前でした。ベトジェットではオンラインでチェックインが出来ないため、チェックインカウンターに30分並び、出国審査も大行列で40分並び、荷物検査もこれまた大行列でさらに40分並びました。
人が多く、電子化もされていないので、とにかく並びます。空港へは早すぎるくらい早く行ったほうが良いです。これまで50カ国以上の空港へ行きましたが、ここまで出入国に時間がかかったのは初めてでした。

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Elios Banner
text:
Aéroport épouvantable avec des indications en anglais minimales. Pas de savon dans les toilettes, des tarifs exorbitants pour manger de la m... Et si vous êtes fumeurs, vous vous sentirez comme un individu de seconde zone avec des zones réservées dégueulasses, minuscules et non aérées. Une honte.

Terrible airport with minimal English language signs. No soap in the toilets, exorbitant prices for eating crap. And if you are a smoker, you will feel like a second-class individual with disgusting, tiny, unventilated designated areas. A disgrace

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: fu hwa Lim
text: This is the worst airport I have ever experienced. Upon arrival, immigration takes at least an hour to get through. Departure is just as bad, with another hour wasted at immigration and security. The waiting areas are overcrowded, stuffy, and exhausting, making it hard to breathe. However, if you’re willing to pay, you can skip the lines with a fast-track service. Here, efficiency isn’t a necessity for travelers—it’s a privilege you have to buy.

text:
Longest lines I’ve ever seen, but it does pass by fairly quick. My problem with this airport though, is that they don’t accept all major credit cards. Furthermore, they don’t accept “dinged up” bills without a penalty.. It’s ridiculous in my opinion how you don’t have options and then they are picky about how pristine your bills are.

The food is alright - definitely better in some stalls than others.. So be on the lookout for the busier stalls!

Restrooms weren’t really clean when I went. Overall a meh experience.

Airport food is also crazily overpriced. I love Vietnam but this airport really makes me think twice before revisiting.

name: Viktoria Stekolnikova
text:
Плохая работа сотрудников аэропорта! Очереди на паспортном контроле просто нереального размера! А на досмотре как будто ещё больше, т.к. все стоят в две змейки! От момента прибытия в аэропорт до момента посадки на борт самолёта, ушло 2 часа и все это время мы стояли в очередях! Успели за 5 минут до отправления автобуса. Впервые такой негативный опыт!
В одном из женских туалетов не было мыло, совсем, даже его следов былого прибывания....
По всей видимости маленький аэропорт пропускает огромный поток пассажиров, но не предназначен для этого. Однозначно надо срочно что-то менять!!!

name: dont break
text:
Come in advance. Getting from the start of the line for immigration check to the end of security check took more than an hour. Plus around 40 minutes at the airlines check in desk. Also, the restrooms are not clean at all.
The good thing is there’s a baggage room. It costs 27k dong per hour or 270k per 10+hours.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: CHUCKII Dkofi
text: The airport seemed understaffed for proper management of immigration and visa services. Poor sinage for the visa processing area, no pens, and the immigration processing worker, was clearly overwhelmed with also being the cashier. No one seemed to be helpful to tourists, and I am thankful I had a pen and Vietnam dong, otherwise it would've been an even longer ordeal. It's interesting, cell phone booths with aggressive salespersons are staged right alongside immigration processing. The sales booths  partially block sight of visa processing area. Moreover, I hope the airport reengineers it's business processes, trains employees to be hospitable, properly staffs it's operations, and updates it's signage while removing the phone sales people. Vietnam, is a great place to visit. The people, the food, the nature, the cities, the country, etc. It's easy to see why Vietnam is becoming a popular tourist location and it's great for the country's economy.

Worst airport I have ever been to.

Immigration took 90 minutes to get through - this seems to be quite regularly the case from other reviews.

The signage is appalling, not clear direction where to go for connecting flights. Ended up having to ask random passengers as the staff were really unhelpful too.

If you have to connect flights through here, research how to connect to your flight in advance and give 3 hours at least between connecting flights!

text:
Definitely a disgusting airport with disgusting service. It's very unpleasant when your luggage is left outside during a heavy rain, things get so wet that you can wring out your entire travel backpack . After that, the equipment broke down, clothes get bad smell and when we tried to somehow resolve the situation, there was no response. Not their problem. From there we flew to Manila, where we had an overweight bag because they treated our luggage so carelessly. The overweight bag was 9 kg due to wet clothes. I hope this review will help others. Be sure to cover your travel backpacks with waterproof fabric
Never again to this airport!

Rude staff everywhere, what a contrast to the super nice Vietnamese you meet on the streets  😂

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Austin Ngo
text:
I'm Viet, but I was so disappointed with this airport. There are so many cons:
- Having to take a bus to get to the plane, and vice versa.
- There is no queue system (or support system such as barriers) nor queue etiquette. People will form multiple lines and will get ahead of you if you're too nice.
- Don't travel in summer because ACs are non-existent.
- If they say boarding time is X, you can add 30 mins on top and the gate probably still won't be opened.
- The food options inside the terminal are just outrageously overpriced.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Alexander Wilson
text: The worst airport in south east Asia, no doubt. We arrived 2,5 hours before our flight, and only just made it. No time for going to the toilet or anything. Just queuing for 2,5 hours straight. First check-in, then immigration and lastly the security check. Nothing works, everything is stupid slow.

Когда предложил сразу оплатить и все, сказал нет, надо сначала деньги что бы выехать, а как приедем тогда и оплачивать. Вобщем кидалы) . Фото самого чела и его машины прикладываю. Обходите стороной,

There were barely any staff around to help, and the whole process felt chaotic. If you’re flying out, make sure you arrive at least 3 hours early or risk missing your flight. One of the worst airport experiences I’ve had.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: ngocduyen nguyenthi
text:
trải nghiệm thực sự rất tệ. mình ở miền tây muốn bay ra nước ngoài nên cực chẳng đã mới
đến sân bay tân sơn nhất. sân bay lúc nào cũng đông nghịch người, lượng khách hàng đông nhưng k tăng cường nhân viên hay tìm cách giải quyết. mình bay chuyến đến Dubai, đã ra sân bay trước gần 3 tiếng rưỡi. nhưng check in xong thì chỉ còn khoảng 30p nữa la đến giờ máy bay mở cửa. còn phải xếp hàng chờ kiểm hộ chiếu ( đông nghịch người) xếp xong thì tiếp tục xếp hàng qua cổng an ninh, cũng đông nghịch người. nhân viên sân bay thì làm việc chậm rãi, từ tốn. bởi vì k phải họ bay nên họ không nôn nóng. mình thì vội. phải chạy đi hỏi hết nhân viên sân bay này đến kia xem có giúp được không. hỏi nhân viên của hãng mình bay (emirates) thì chị nhân viên thờ ơ bảo " vẫn phải xếp hàng thôi, khi nao máy bay sắp đóng cửa thì tiếp viên sẽ tìm khách hàng, lúc đó chị cố lắng nghe tên của mình rồi giơ tay lên...".?? giơ tay lên trong biển người đông nghịch??. mình đành chạy đi hỏi người khác. tìm được phương án thì mình đi gọi bạn trai mình ( 2 người bay chung) đến để cùng đi, vậy mà còn bị nhân viên sân bay đang ngồi ghế thong dong mỉa mai, "hễ cho một đứa ưu tiên la nó kéo theo cả lũ". mình k hiểu nổi luôn. một nhân viên mà có thể dùng từ như vậy nói với khách hàng. lúc đó mình vội quá nên k thèm tranh cải làm gì, do chuyến bay mình 23:10 mở cửa mà lúc đó là 23:20 rồi. bạn trai mình lần đầu về Việt Nam
cũng nói đúng là để lại ấn tượng vô cùng tệ hại với Sân bay tân sơn nhất.

text: Good terminal but the method of aligthing of aircraft for transfer flights have downtime..😉

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Martine
text:
Le passage à l'émigration pour entrer dans le pays est problématique,  pour les vols arrivent  tous les passagers débarquent en même temps et la file d'attente est très longue, nous avons attendu plus d'une heure, aucun siège pour s'asseoir. C'est irrespectueux pour les personnes âgées et les femmes enceintes .
Nous avions un autre vol à prendre à l'aéroport domestique heureusement ce vol avait du retard sinon nous l'aurions raté.
Aucune aide de la part du personnel.
Le passage à l'émigration est plus simple à la sortie, les passagers arrivant progressivement.
Pas de Wi-Fi dans l'aéroport.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: 金森慎太郎
text: コーヒー等はホーチミン市内のスーパー等で買うのと比べて割高でオススメできません。航空会社のラウンジも小さめで、イマイチです。

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: 卿.
text:
第一次自行訂機票沒經驗，沒有按照護照上的名字訂票，導致回程時在機場遇到了一連串的麻煩。

出發時的順利讓我們掉以輕心，在小港機場辦理 check-in 時發現名字錯誤，地勤與疑似主管確認後，幫我們改正了。我們以為問題解決，便安心展開旅程。

人潮、拖延與名字錯誤的三重打擊，回國當天班機14:25起飛；抵達台灣18:30，我們提前在十點半左右抵達越南胡志明機場，發現 check-in 櫃檯大排長龍，但辦理速度卻非常慢。等到 11:03 終於輪到我們，地勤卻告知要優先處理前一班航班的旅客，要我們 30 分鐘後再來排隊，想說時間還夠，於是先去吃午餐，順便喝杯咖啡。

約 11:35 回來重新排隊，發現原定 14:25 的航班已延誤至 14:45。但這還不是最糟的——當我們終於輪到時，地勤告知機票上的名字與護照不符。我們以為在台灣時已經修改完成，沒想到還是出錯了。

地勤一問三不知，處理過程極為混亂，當我們詢問是否可以現場更改時，地勤小姐卻表示：「不知道，沒用過」，甚至直接建議我們買新的機票比較快！聽到翻譯轉述這句話，我心裡瞬間火冒三丈。

經過多次溝通，她終於去詢問旁邊的同事，但對方也不清楚，只叫我們自己去服務台詢問。到了服務台，才得知可以付費 6 萬越盾更改名字。處理完畢後，我們又重新排隊辦理 check-in，整個過程耗時超過兩個小時，站到腳都酸了。

好不容易完成 check-in，進到登機口卻發現，原定 13:50 報到的時間，直到 14:20 才開放登機。接著又站著等接駁車，直到快 15:00 才真正登機。然而，不明原因再次導致延誤最終15:33 才起飛。

糟糕的體驗，學到了寶貴教訓
這次旅程，從訂票錯誤到地勤處理不當，再到航班延誤，各種突發狀況接連不斷。最重要的教訓是：訂票時一定要確保名字與護照完全一致，否則後續可能會遇到無數麻煩。另外，在效率較低的機場，一定要預留更多時間，以免因突發狀況影響行程。

雖然這次經歷不佳，但也算是一次寶貴的學習機會，希望未來的旅行能更順利！

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Danila Spaliansky
text:
Review of Tan Son Nhat International Airport (SGN) – Ho Chi Minh City

Tan Son Nhat International Airport (SGN) is Vietnam’s busiest airport, handling both domestic and international flights. Here’s a breakdown of my experience:

Pros:

✅ Location: The airport is relatively close to the city center (about 6-8 km), making it convenient to reach by taxi or ride-hailing services.
✅ Facilities: There are plenty of restaurants, cafés, and duty-free shops, especially in the international terminal. Free WiFi is available, and it generally works well.
✅ Immigration Efficiency: While immigration can be slow during peak hours, it is generally well-organized, and e-visa processing is straightforward.
✅ Lounge Options: Several lounges offer a comfortable escape from the crowded terminals, with decent food and drinks.

Cons:

❌ Overcrowding: The airport is often extremely busy, with limited seating in waiting areas and long lines at check-in, security, and immigration.
❌ Limited Vegetarian Food Options: Finding good vegetarian meals can be a challenge, especially in the domestic terminal.
❌ Taxi Scams: Some taxi drivers outside the airport try to overcharge. It’s best to use Grab or go to the official taxi stand to avoid issues.

Tips for Travelers:
•	Arrive Early: Security and immigration can take a while, so give yourself extra time, especially for international flights.
•	Use Grab or Vinasun Taxis: These are the most reliable options to avoid scams.
•	Bring Snacks: If you’re vegetarian or have dietary restrictions, having your own food is a good idea.

Overall, Tan Son Nhat Airport is functional but crowded, so patience is key. It’s best to be prepared for long lines, and if you can, book lounge access for a more comfortable experience.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Nguyễn Mạnh Giáp
text:
Tan Son Nhat Airport (IATA: SGN, ICAO: VVTS) is the largest international airport in Vietnam, located in Tan Binh District, Ho Chi Minh City. It is the most important aviation gateway in the southern region, serving both domestic and international flights.

Key Information:
•	Design capacity: Around 28 million passengers per year (but often exceeds this limit in reality).
•	Terminals:
•	Domestic Terminal (T1): Serves domestic flights.
•	International Terminal (T2): Serves international flights.
•	Terminal T3 (under construction): Expected to be completed in 2025 to ease congestion.
•	Runways:
•	07L/25R: 3,048 meters long.
•	07R/25L: 3,800 meters long (the longest runway in Vietnam).
•	Location: About 8 km from downtown Ho Chi Minh City, making it convenient for travel.
•	Major airlines: Vietnam Airlines, VietJet Air, Bamboo Airways, Pacific Airlines, along with many international airlines.

Key Issues:
•	Overload: The airport frequently operates beyond capacity, especially during peak seasons.
•	Traffic congestion: Surrounding roads such as Truong Son and Cong Hoa are often heavily congested.
•	Expansion & Improvements: Projects are underway to expand the airport, including the construction of Terminal T3 and infrastructure upgrades.

기다리기싫은분들이 패스트트랙신청해서 나오시는듯(3.5만원가량)

한번에최대인출금액은 200만동이라고해서 200만동씩 여러번인출했어요
- 그랩존은 5-6번에서 길건너편으로가서 초록옷입은분들 찾아가면됩니다.

text: This airport is by far the worst I’ve ever experienced. They don’t allow anyone without a boarding pass to stay inside, which means if I wanted to see my family off before their flight, I wouldn’t be able to accompany them beyond the entrance. What kind of policy is that? On top of that, the security staff are rude and unprofessional, speaking to customers in a dismissive manner without even bothering to explain the rules. They treat foreign travelers the same way they do locals, without any effort to provide clear information. At the very least, they should be able to properly communicate the reason behind their policies. Without a doubt, this is the worst airport I’ve encountered.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: 劉育志
text:
胡志明市新山國際機場與胡志明市國內線在隔壁，但無法裡面互通，需要出境之後互相走外面聯外道路即可互相抵達。G樓層互通往漢堡王方向走即可到達國內線大樓。

**國際線入境  (進入越南)  G樓層

下機後跟著人群走，開始排隊過海關，建議如果同時抵達班機不多，趕快去排隊過海關(旁邊兩側會有換錢處以及賣網卡，建議先排海關出去再購買)，如果有托運行李旅客，其實不需要辦理快速通關或是塞錢快速通海關，因為領行會花非常久時間等候，行李很慢才會領到。
給海關的資料 (護照、出發地的機票、電子簽證)，一起拿給海關等候，(戶外)很多人舉牌接機的地方才是真正的走出機場。

**國際線出境  (離開越南)  2樓

出境在樓上，如果出境搭的車子司機未送達此樓層，請於入境的G樓層漢堡王商店旁邊有一個小門，搭乘電梯或是手扶梯上樓。
出境航空公司似乎2.5小時前就開櫃了 (長榮、華航 ，其它家航空不確定)，長榮航空有分線上已報到或是線上未報到的排隊，排錯櫃台勤務人員會解釋幾句，但還是會幫你完成手續。

拿完機票，走到中間後面排隊過海關出境，人潮很多需要預留多一點時間，因為海關速度也不快，會花很多時間出關，海關過後就是隨身安檢，此時需要刷機票條碼，然後安檢需要脫手錶、鞋子，穿拖鞋也是需要掃X光機，所以請記得脫鞋。

備註:
1.越南機場漢堡王超級貴，咖啡、椰子商品，食物伴手禮建議市區超市買，因為機場比市區貴2倍。

2.國內線入境  G樓層
下機後跟著人群走，等提領行李即可走出國內線機場，走出大門左手邊沿著人行通道，即可到達國際機場。

此經驗提供給來越南玩的旅客參考  ！！
by  09/FEB/2025經驗

The business gate to Vietnam, full of foreign investments makes such a horrible impression on guests. Anyone who manages this should be dismissed. text:

The restroom was very unfriendly. The men's restroom was full. I couldn't bear the pain in my stomach, so I went to the accessible restroom. When I came out, I was scolded that the accessible restroom was only for women and children. I had to wait no matter whether the men's restroom was full or not. What kind of service attitude was this?

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: ぱにパニックぱに
text:
Water costs $6, and pho with a drink costs $19—these prices are outrageously high.
If a vending machine for drinks were installed, there would surely be a long queue.

The chairs are broken, and charging devices is difficult.

It’s absurd that the staff are occupying the seats instead of customers.

A reasonable pricing system and responsible staff are needed.

Nước có giá 6 đô, còn phở và đồ uống có giá 19 đô—giá quá đắt!
Nếu có một máy bán nước tự động, chắc chắn sẽ có hàng dài người xếp hàng.

Ghế thì hỏng, và việc sạc thiết bị cũng rất bất tiện.

Thật vô lý khi nhân viên lại chiếm chỗ ngồi thay vì khách hàng.

Cần có một chính sách giá hợp lý và đội ngũ nhân viên có trách nhiệm.

name: Giang Huynh
text: The airport is very crowded today, which is the issue that needs a long term solution. But I want to give 5 star to the check in crew, especially Trân. I asked for a change of seat and they could not find one as the plane is full. When I was waiting at the waiting area (1h later), they called my name and gave me a new boarding pass, telling me they have managed to find one seat that meet my ask. This is to my greatest surprise, because they can remember the customer need out of many hundreds, and followed up. My seat is economy by the way. The Vietnamese check in crew is amazing. Thanks a lot for this. Thank you Trân for a very personalised great experience.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Jennifer J
text:
Wir sind hier umgestiegen, es war sehr chaotisch. Da wir einen Airlinewechsel hatten Quatar -> Vietnam Airlines war nicht klar, ob die Koffer automatisch umtransportiert werden. Die Frage kam erst auf, als wir über unsere Koffer "stolperten" auf der Suche nach dem Gate. Viele Fluggäste hatten sich bereits ihre Koffer geschnappt und haben diese erneut eingecheckt. Was wir auch taten, da der Mitarbeiter selbst nicht genau wusste was mit den Koffern passiert.

Nach dem Aussteigen aus dem Flugzeug mussten wir in eine sehr langen Schlange für die passkontrolle. Danach muss man aus dem Gebäude(mit den Koffern (die stehen unten bei den Treppen)), dann geht man ein Stück zum nächsten Gebäude spazieren.. eigentlich geht man bis zum Ende des Flughafen um die Koffer wieder einzuchecken und seine Flugtickets abzuholen. Danach geht man völlig gestresst zum Gate und bekommt vielleicht noch seinen Flug. Zwei Stunden sollte man schon locker einplanen zum Umsteigen und sich zurecht zu finden. Die Beschilderung gibt es auch teilweise nicht, oder nur ein Schild. Zu den Gates geht es über eine versteckte Rolltreppe. Die WCs sind auch eher solala.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: K Dang
text:
Strangely, this small airport has become my favorite airport of all time! I've been all over the world. Sure, the airport is small and dingy, but the people is so courteous and helpful.

On my way to VN, the airline lost my luggage. The Lost and Found team helped me find my luggage in 30 min despite me not having my boarding pass anymore (I later discovered it was in my pocket the whole time). And they were so nice!!! I usually loath dealing with airport staff.

On my way out of VN, I accidentally had an forbidden item in my carry on. Genuine mistake, it's a common item not allowed. The customs staff, to my surprise, was so helpful and nice! They accompanied my brother inside the airport to retrieve the item.

I was amazed at how friendly and helpful VN people have been at this airport.

Worst airport I've ever seen. And Vietjet is the worst airline. Queues are pointlessly ridiculous. Worse than Luton. They make you queue at the check-in counter even if you've checked in online and you don't have any checkin bags.

40 mins at check in counter and then another 40 at passport control to check my passport LEAVING Vietnam. Why do they care so much about checking who is leaving their country?

Prices for food and drink are also exorbitant.

It's enough to put anyone off visiting Vietnam.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Sean Lin
text:
越南機場設備老舊且較為落後，直接影響出入境旅客的通關時間。大排長龍的情況可能源於電腦系統效率不佳，而越南方面目前並未提出相應對策來改善。

若想加快通關速度，某些旅客會選擇「小費」方式，也就是變相的賄賂。不少熟悉情況的旅客，為了節省排隊時間，會主動在護照內夾帶現金，以順利加快入境或出境流程。

Vietnamese airport facilities are outdated and underdeveloped, directly impacting the time it takes for travelers to pass through immigration. Long queues may be caused by inefficient computer systems, and currently, Vietnam has no effective measures in place to address this issue.

For those who want to expedite the immigration process, offering a “tip” – essentially a form of bribery – is a common practice. Many experienced travelers, in order to avoid long waiting times, discreetly place cash inside their passports to facilitate a faster entry or exit.

ベトナムの空港設備は老朽化しており、発展も遅れているため、出入国手続きにかかる時間に直接影響を及ぼしています。長蛇の列ができる原因の一つとして、コンピューターシステムの効率の悪さが考えられますが、現在のところ、ベトナム側はこれに対する有効な対策を講じていません。

出入国をスムーズにしたい場合、一部の旅行者は「チップ」を渡すことを選びます。これは事実上の賄賂と言えます。事情をよく知る旅行者の多くは、長時間並ぶのを避けるため、パスポートの中に現金を挟み、より早く入出国できるようにしています。

베트남 공항의 시설은 노후화되고 낙후되어 있어, 출입국 절차에 걸리는 시간이 직접적으로 영향을 받습니다. 긴 줄이 생기는 원인은 비효율적인 컴퓨터 시스템 때문일 가능성이 높으며, 현재 베트남 측에서는 이에 대한 뚜렷한 해결책을 마련하지 않고 있습니다.

출입국 절차를 빠르게 진행하고 싶다면, 일부 여행객들은 일종의 “팁”을 제공하는 방법을 선택합니다. 사실상 뇌물과 다름없습니다. 이를 잘 알고 있는 많은 여행객들은 긴 대기 시간을 피하기 위해 여권 속에 현금을 끼워 넣고 보다 신속하게 입·출국 절차를 진행합니다.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Stacey Marshall
text:
Arrived 4 hours before international flight. Check-in opened three hours before, was able to check in quickly and then queued for passport check (immigration) and then queued again for security check. Had to unpack electronics, take off shoes and watch., belt, etc. Took us just over an hour to get through all checks.
Once through thought that we’d spend our remaining Dong. However as with most airports prices are very high. As an example a Chocolate Freeze from Highlands Coffee is 100,000 which is 31,000 more than advertised outside the airport. And a can of soft drink was 55,000, only 20,000 outside. Same with confectionery and gifts. The shelves are well stocked, and hardly anyone buying.  So shall be taking Dong to currency exchange - which there is here at the airport.
There are several water points for filling up your empty bottles, and there is hot water too.   Toilets were kept clean, as was the airport in general.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Caleb Smith
text:
Airport feels very dated. The positive is there are good food options that are pretty quiet, well priced and relaxing. The remaining portion of the airport is overwhelmingly hot (in January), has nearly zero charging locations (I found 2 in the entire airport with long queues), have some of the slowest passport control lines I've ever seen outside of America (still faster than going back to the US) and dirty/poorly maintained. It doesn't seem like a good look for welcoming someone to your country, but I have been to worse airports. Expect long queues, outdated checkin procedures, requiring you get a paper ticket, and a need for a battery bank (which can't be rented onsite).

Also, be mindful of the scammy taxi drivers trying to push you into their vehicles and away from Grab, which is safer and more affordable. I even had a driver tell me he'd "help me find my Grab driver" before he tried to shove me in his car and demanded money, then yelled at me when I told him I already have a driver booked. This kind of hostility should be illegal at the airport, which led to negative opinions of the area before even seeing the city. Be warned, the taxi drivers are scammers and apparently the city allows it.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Aron Sarmiento
text:
This review is to warn and protect fellow tourists!

We were in Ho Chi Minh City airport, and after collecting our luggage, we were approached from all sides by people offering SIM cards and taxis.
A taxi driver initially offered us a ride for 400k, which we declined. Another driver then offered 125k, and we agreed.

The driver insisted that I sit in the front while my friend sat in the back but not directly behind the driver. When I reached for money to pay, the taxi broker tried to grab my cash. I demanded it back, and luckily he didn’t manage to steal anything at that point.

However, the money was passed to the taxi driver, who managed to steal some Euro notes. I confronted my friend, asking if he saw what happened, but he claimed he didn’t and even yelled at me, making me doubt myself. The whole situation felt suspicious.

They then drove us to a taxi station and forced us to switch taxis. Feeling uneasy, I handed my wallet to my friend to double-check. That’s when we realized some Swiss Francs were also missing.

When we demanded the driver stop and threatened to call the police, they returned part of the stolen money. My friend also demanded the Swiss Francs back, but the driver had the audacity to ask if we wouldn’t “tip” him. I recorded everything on video.

We got out of the taxi and ordered a Grab instead.

Before our return flight, we decided to report the incident to the airport police. We showed them the video footage, and within five minutes, they brought the two thieves and their boss to the station. However, it became clear that the police were working with them.

The “policeman” and the taxi drivers were laughing before entering the room. It was all a show. The officer asked for my passport twice, even though he didn’t check it properly the first time. He wasted over an hour, fully aware of how long the immigration process at the airport in Vietnam takes.

At the station, the boss promised to return all the stolen money if we stopped involving the police. We knew it was a lie but didn’t want to risk missing our flight due to this corrupt charade.

Once outside, we confronted the boss again, and his group of 4-5 people started yelling, showing us the middle finger, and threatening us. Instead of feeling intimidated, we couldn’t stop laughing, which made them angrier. They resorted to insults, but we just grabbed our things and left to catch our flight.

This experience was unsettling and shows how some individuals, including corrupt authorities, prey on tourists. Be extremely cautious in Ho Chi Minh City, especially when taking taxis. Use trusted services like Grab, and don’t let your guard down.

name: Anh Lục
text:
Thái độ của nhân viên tạp vụ nhà vệ sinh nữ cổng
19 lên máy bay rất không tốt, nay 26/1/2025 có ghé nhà vệ sinh, 2 phòng đầu trống,1 bồn đầy giấy,
1 bồn vàng khè lè, tưởng nhà vệ sinh tắc ra hỏi "cô ơi nhà vệ sinh tắc hả cô, con thấy từa lưa ở đó", bã làm cho một tràng "mấy người đi không xối chứ ai, đi không xối chạy ra kêu ai, ai làm tắc mà kêu tui,....", mình vô gạt nước, giấy không trôi đi, ra báo
"cô ơi, hình như bị tắc thật, con gạt rồi giấy còn ở
", bã chạy vô" sọt không bỏ, bỏ từa lưa vô bồn cầu, chờ phòng đối diện trống đi đi” Rồi ha, vừa vô
phòng đối diện vừa có người đi ra kia, nghe bã nói chuyện với ai đó kiểu xỉa xói "đi không xối, sồn sồn lên kêu tắc, tắc cái đầu mày chứ tắc,..." Ủa alo, đã sử dụng nhà vệ sinh đó đâu, lớn già đầu rồi, người ta dùng ngữ khí rất bình thường để hỏi, rồi mắc gì lườm liếc rồi đổ thừa người ta (lúc đi ra thấy bã ngồi ăn mì, ngang qua bã còn liếc) ai bỏ giấy, ai làm 1 bôn thè lè đó đi mà kiếm chửi, kiếm liếc đi, người gì đâu không!

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Sam Senai
text:
ホーチミン市内中心部から、車で約30分。アクセスは悪くないのですが、
世界中で、過去に約100前後の国際空港を利用しましたが、残念ながら、ここが過去最悪の空港です。
入国審査、出国審査、手荷物検査、手荷物受取、いずれも大行列でかなりの時間がかかります。出国時はチェックインカウンターがopenする3時間前には到着することをオススメします（2時間前の到着ですとギリギリです）。
国際線で到着後、ベトナム国内線の乗り継ぎもかなり時間がかかる場合があるので乗り継ぎ時間には余裕をもって予定を組みましょう。
ベトナム自体は良い国で人々も素晴らしいのに、ここ空港のおかげで印象が悪くなります。
1日でも早く改善した方が良いと思います。

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: jjdkd
text: Flughafen ist vom Aufbau und von den Essensmöglichkeiten ganz in Ordnung aber die Wartezeiten bei der Ein- und Ausreise sind katastrophal. Gerade die Einreise hat immenses Verbesserungspotenzial, da sie nicht nur mehr als 2,5 Stunden gedauert hat, sondern auch kein ausreichendes Personenleitsystem vorhanden war, sodass sich sehr viele Leute vorgedrängelt haben. Außerdem war die Mentalität vieler Wartender, dass es schneller vorangeht, wenn man den Vordermann dauerhaft anrempelt und weiter nach vorne schiebt, gerade hier wäre ein Leitsystem von Vorteil gewesen.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Tom
text:
Diese 1 Sterne Bewertung bezieht sich auf den Einreise- und Ausreise Vorgang. Zu jeder Zeit kommt es an den Passkontrollen zu Wartezeiten um die 2 Std .
Es scheint als sei der Flughafen auf das große Passagieraufkommen dieser Tage nicht mehr ausgelegt. Da bedarf es dringend Verbesserung.
Im Security Bereich waren nicht alle Schleusen geöffnet, sodass man sich auch hier wieder in eine Warteschlange einreihen darf. Im Einreisebereich waren nur die Hälfte der Schalter besetzt.
Alles in allem sollte man für einen internationalen Flug mindestens 4-5std vorher am Flughafen sein.

name: Mạnh
text:
Trời ơi, sáng nay suýt chút nữa là mình lỡ chuyến bay, quá là đông luôn á.
Nói không ngoa chứ đông nghẹt như chợ Tết, người người chen chúc nhau. Mình phải len lỏi, luồn lách mãi mới tới được quầy check-in.
Loanh quanh làm thủ tục xong xuôi thì cũng gần đến giờ bay rồi, tim đập thình thịch. Chạy hộc tốc qua cổng an ninh, may mà kịp giờ, thở không ra hơi.
Chắc tại hôm nay là 25 Tết, ai cũng tranh thủ về quê ăn Tết nên sân bay mới đông khủng khiếp vậy. Mình thì rút kinh nghiệm sâu sắc, lần sau phải ra sân bay sớm hơn nữa mới được! 😩

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: yuchen
text:
感覺還有待改進的機場，等待落地簽的時間冗長，連入境也都需要大排長龍，而且海關人員也是慢慢來，看起來都像快睡著了，也沒有像新加坡有快速通關系統，這對自由行的遊客來說很不方便，幾乎所有人都並不喜歡這樣的入境體驗！而且過程中還看到很多人在插隊，情何以堪啊，現在都能在網路上先用好，節省大家的時間，越南網上有很多收費標準不一的自辦簽證，自己旅行時買的話也要小心。
***👽最後重要的是，要小心機場的不良計程車！或許在機場內叫到的沒問題，或者確認是搭車系統本人的車。我在使用Grab 時，由於系統給的上車位置不是我要的，我被引導走到機場外停車場附近，徒步過去，卻有人問我有無叫車。我心想怎麼會有人知道我叫車，拒絕了一個後，又來一個男子，他好心給我秀出Grab 系統，由於是來到東南亞才下載，並不太熟悉操作，之前有坐過多是Grab的機車，這個跳出來的男子，引導我去停車地方，然後假裝陪我等不到幾秒鐘，還打電話給我訂的grab，車子同樣是白色，他跟對方用越南語通話後，跟我說司機繞不進來....
我心想難道不是你，你帶我來停車場做什麼？後來問我欲訂的Grab是多少錢，他說一樣價錢就可以，帶我到我的旅館。 時間是下班尖峰，又下著雨，等車比較難，不然本來想搭公車到市區，多年以前也曾經搭過。 後來搭他的車，他會不停的搭話，顯得友善，然後到達地點後，本來只要120,0000左右，他喊700,000越南盾。還不包括從停車場出來說規定都要給機場停車場的錢。 所以結論是小心主動跟你搭訕的計程車，最好都用店家叫車或確認手機系統找到的車，不然就是大眾運輸，絕對不要搭私人車。也別聽信一樣價錢這種話，在東南亞的落後國家，熱鬧地區的不肖騙子對外國人其實一點都不友善，太友善的也都別信會吃虧，特別要多留意。這些人就是他們自己國家的毒瘤，壞了大家的印象，噁心他們自己國家的門面。而且越南、泰國、菲律賓都有類似的情況...

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: TP Fam
text: I’m truly disappointed that the international airport in one of Vietnam’s largest cities is in worse condition than others, such as those in Danang and Phu Quoc. Not only are the facilities outdated, but the service staff on the landside are also underwhelming. While I acknowledge that Long Thanh International Airport is set to open soon, I sincerely hope they invest in better staff training, as these employees will represent the country when visitors arrive. Honestly, Tan Son Nhat Airport feels more like a crowded marketplace than a modern international hub. Additionally, I’m perplexed by the poor grooming of some staff, especially those handling our luggage or walking around the airport, it comes across as very unprofessional.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Ness Phan
text:
The Worst Airport for Families Traveling with Young Children

Tan Son Nhat Airport is, without a doubt, the worst airport I’ve ever experienced. It shows no humanity or sympathy, especially for families traveling with young children. As a family of five with three young kids aged 2, 4, and 8, I expected some level of priority or assistance—something that is standard in most airports outside of Vietnam.

Everywhere else, there are clear priority lines for families with young children, often with staff present to direct you to those lines. But at Tan Son Nhat, the focus seems to be entirely on selling their fast-track service. For a fee, you can “buy” priority access. Is this normal? Where does this money go? It certainly isn’t being used to improve the airport’s facilities or address its chronic inefficiency.

The airport is always overcrowded, poorly organised, and severely understaffed. Queues often take at least an hour, with no consideration for families struggling to manage young children in such conditions. It’s a terrible experience, and I genuinely believe this is one of the worst airports on the planet.

Instead of prioritising profit from fast-track services, perhaps some of that effort should go toward making the airport functional and humane for all passengers—especially those with young children.

But guess what you need the printed boarding pass so be aware of this, is easy to get deceived if you are not aware of these rules, and you can lose long time queueing for migration to just get send back to the counters which probably will mean losing your flight. 👍🏻 Be aware! Btw Scoot staff were very unfriendly and disrespectful towards me, unfortunately I was not able to record the guy who yelled at me saying it was my fault, though clearly they have misleading info on their boarding pass

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Exploring 4 Enjoyment
text:
Tan Son Nhat Airport Experience:

1.	Crowded and Inefficient Service
The airport is consistently busy, resulting in long wait times for several processes:

•	Check-in: Even with online check-in, expect a lengthy queue at the counter.
•	Immigration: Long lines are unavoidable, causing delays.
•	Luggage Screening: Another area where wait times are significant.

Avoid purchasing the Fast Track service, as it offers little to no value. Passengers are merely directed to the crew/VIP line and often left unattended. There is a risk of being removed by staff if they discover you’re not eligible. Additionally, Fast Track does not expedite the luggage screening process, making it an unnecessary expense.
2.	Overpriced Food and Beverages
The cost of food and drinks within the airport is extremely high. For example, a 500ml bottle of water can cost $2–$3. A more economical option is the 7-Eleven store located in the parking area basement, which offers reasonable prices.
3.	Taxi Services
Avoid taxis at the arrival area or individuals offering rides. These services are notorious for overcharging, often demanding 3–4 times the standard fare, and they may withhold change.

Recommendations:
•	Use the Grab app for a reliable and fair-priced ride.
•	If Grab is unavailable, opt for trusted taxi companies such as Mai Linh Taxi or Vinasun Taxi, both of which operate with metered fares.

By following these tips, you can navigate the airport experience more efficiently and avoid unnecessary expenses or inconveniences.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Christopher W.
text:
Diese 1 Sterne Bewertung bezieht sich auf den Einreise- und Ausreise Vorgang. Zu Stoßzeiten kann es an den Passkontrollen schnell zu Wartezeiten an die 1-2h kommen.
Es scheint als sei der Flughafen auf das große Passagieraufkommen dieser Tage nicht mehr ausgelegt. Da bedarf es dringend Verbesserung.
Im Security Bereich waren nicht alle Schleusen geöffnet, sodass man sich auch hier wieder in eine Warteschlange einreihen darf.
Alles in allem sollte man für einen internationalen Flug mindestens 3-4h vorher am Flughafen sein.
Nach dem Security Bereich gibt es noch genug Möglichkeiten etwas zu essen oder zu trinken sowie Geld zu wechseln.
Dies ist als positiv zu bewerten.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: r.l. resle
text:
ah, Tan Son.

if Dante Alighieri had a chance to fly to Saigon, he wouldn't have had to travel all the way to the depths of hell to write the first and world famous part of his masterpiece "The Divine Comedy".

Because this, this is a place of tortured souls and brutal punishment. Take for instance the sole picture I'll provide of this malignant location: the "waiting  area" of the international - yes, international terminal. Consisting of a few rows of plastic seats conveniently arranged OUTSIDE the terminal itself. Because as we all know, weather in Saigon is gentle and refreshing.

You will sweat, you will look for help and find demands to take off this and that. You will search for refreshments and find cash only machines. You will find no trace of automation. You will find lines of 300 people at th immigration counters, with three solitary officers painstakingly processing one passport every 3-4 minutes. The math is all yours to do.

This torturous place is meant to be the hub to a bustling city of 10+ million people and growing. This place of gnashing teeth and shrieks of pain is intendend as the welcoming face of this rapidly evolving economy. This grotesque assembly of unfinished, flimsy, or just plain antiquated facilities is supposed to be an airport.

As the song they play in a loop of VietJet airlines to add torture to torture, we can all only hope:-

"One day..."

name: SS H
text:
Update 2025. Be super cautious when exchanging money at the airport. SCAM!

Still seeing some of money exchange booths at the airport are still trying to scam tourists by giving less money, intentionally. For example, when the exchange remainder is 300,000, they would intentionally give you 30,000 and see if you notice or not, as some people would not think too much after counting the big ones (500,000) already or some people missing the idea of how many 0 of VND should be. This was a common trick many years ago. Couldn’t believe people are still doing this in 2025.

Count slowly, use calculator in full digit, do not omit 3 digit (1,000). That’s what they would show you to confuse you.

Some tiny people at this international airport of the good country Vietnam are doing their job to lose the country’s face at many tourists’ first day of arrival. What a shame!

2023/12: was luck enough to go thru custom green line this time because i guess I just had carry-on with me. Still saw officers ask other people to go thru red line if they had big luggages.

Very inefficient at immigration and custom. Not tourist friendly country. There is “no goods to declare” line but every passenger still has to go thru X-ray anyway. Then please remove the fake lines. You don’t see any enthusiasm from the officers to welcome you to enter their country!

text:
With Vietjet airline, in this airport I had the worst experience in the long time.

I bought the international ticket, checked in online, and printed the ticket I received. With this ticket I stranded half an hour for immigration, where they said that it’s not a ticket (though it was stated clearly in it that it’s a boarding pass, and that I should print it which I did on paper).

This is aweful, as I had to not only come back to check in gate to get the ticket printed by vietjet, but also go through slow and very long immigration line again. They didn’t allow me to cut the line. Then security. I still managed to cut the line myself couple of times. Otherwise I would t make it to the flight.

It was VERY stressful.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: K ing
text: ⭐⭐⭐新山一機場的前身是1930年，法屬印度支那政府在新山一村興建的簡易機場。1956年，駐越美軍授助越南共和國（南越）政府改建空軍基地，新建長7,200-英尺（2,190-公尺）的跑道，成為南越的空運門戶。越南戰爭期間作為美軍空軍駐地，繁忙程度位居世界前列。在越戰的最後階段，泛美航空每周有四次途徑馬尼拉和關島前往舊金山的波音747航線1968年至1974年期間，美國大陸航空每周有多達30架波音707軍用包機往返於新山一機場1975年4月，於430事件中，北越軍隊砲擊機場、美國海軍陸戰隊、美僑及越南難民從機場撤離。兩越統一後，此後，新山一的拼寫由南越式的Tân Sơn Nhứt改為北越式的Tân Sơn Nhất。

text:
Пока что это худший аэропорт, в котором мне довелось побывать
Если у вас пересадка с международного рейса, на внутренний , будьте готовы провести это время на улице или ехать в гостиницу.
Вход во внутренний терминал просто закрывают и открывают только ко времени регистрации. Обменник только в международном терминале. Во внутреннем терминале нет ничего для комфортного ожидания рейса. Грязно и ползают тараканы.
Я в шоке, учитывая, что это не какой то аэропорт в городе с населением 100 000 человек, а один из крупных городов Азии

text:
Aéroport propre. Facile de s’y repérer.
Nous avions réservé un vol avec Bamboo Airways et l’enregistrement ainsi que le dépôt de bagage s’est fait sans problèmes.

Le passage de la sécurité de même, les règles sont un peu strictes: retirer tous l’électronique, les chaussures etc.
Mais on le passe très rapidement et facilement.

Une fois de l’autre côté il y a beaucoup de place pour s’assoir et des petits restaurants où manger qui sont très convenable pour des restaurants d’aéroport.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: f y
text:
日本からホーチミン国際空港に夜到着しました。ホテルへ行くためgrabを予約しました。私は初めてのホーチミン、初めてのgrabです。
現れたドライバーにスマホを見せろと言われ、操作されて何故か助手席に座らされました。怪しいと思いつ、初めてのことでわかりません。クレカ使えないから現金と言われお金の単位がわからず探している時に1,500,000dをすられました。その後急にドライバー変わると言って違うドライバーが現れて、grabに表示されていた金額140,000dで無事に？ホテルの近くで降ろされました。怪しいことが多くありましたが、フレンドリーな雰囲気で警戒心が緩みました。
ホテルに入ってgrabを確認するとクレカがデフォルトになっていて履歴も残っていません。そして財布を確認して、お金をすられたと気づきました。
その後旅行中grab使いましたがとても便利です。助手席に座らされることもありません。
ホーチミンではスリに会うこともありませんでした。
grabは安心と言われますが、空港での初grabにはご注意ください。

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Tran Hoa
text:
Tan Son Nhat Airport
The largest airport in the South, where most international flights take off and land. From here, you can travel to most cities in Vietnam by plane. Currently, TSN airport is overloaded, you should arrive at the airport earlier than usual to avoid traffic jams and long queues. Always let us know in advance if you need help to avoid missing your flight departure time.
The government is planning to open another airport in Long Thanh city, quite far from Ho Chi Minh city, and route all international flights there. At that time, Tan Son Nhat airport will become a domestic airport. Hope the plan goes well to reduce the load on TSN as well as the traffic jam in Ho Chi Minh city

‼️出境機場外的銀行騙錢‼️
大家自己小心一點⋯⋯

600元美金給櫃檯，實際只收到300美金的越南盾
當下美金給櫃檯後，他也沒按計算機給我看
也沒有任何的收據❌ 但金額我很確定是600美金
因為是我特地事前去銀行換來的

我們下飛機已經半夜，可能我們也有點迷糊
所以沒有當場清點數目🫠
加上想說在機場內的銀行，不可能有問題吧！
結果剛到越南第二天，買飲料時發現錢不夠
這時候我們才意識到，是第一天換錢被騙的‼️

因為這兩天所有花費我都有記帳
我們都是走路去任何地方，只有下飛機那天坐一趟車
雖然已上網爬文過，但沒想到機場銀行也會詐騙⋯
來這邊換錢的人要特別留意⚠️

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: H. Almojera
text: Travelling on NY's Day, my arrival at this airport was not an easy experience. But I already expected it to be busy because of the holidays. There were soooo many passengers that it took me 2 hours standing for the immigration queue. I am a Filipino citizen so I got visa exemption for 21 days. Another hour spent at the luggage carousel as several flights arrived almost at the same time. Still thankful that my luggage was not lost nor taken by mistake. Also, just be careful with the people who offer taxi rides because they charge waaayyyy higher than the actual fare. I got warned earlier so it's safer to use the Grab app. My curiosity made me try to ask the price from one driver and the fare difference in the Grab App that time was around 200,000. So don't get scammed. I was able to withdraw from the ATM near the exit using my Mastercard Debit. However, the tourists who tried ahead of me failed to get money using their cards. I am not sure if it's the same case for others. So make sure you have some cash with you. I left the airport 3.5 hours from the time we landed.

text:
Title: A Disastrous Experience at Ho Chi Minh Airport

Ho Chi Minh Airport is, by far, one of the worst airports I’ve ever experienced as a frequent traveler. Here are the main issues I encountered:
1.	Total disorganization at immigration:
Upon arrival, you are forced to wait in an endless line at immigration, with no clear system in place. It’s chaotic and time-consuming.
2.	Unnecessary double waiting lines:
After finally clearing immigration, you face another queue for security checks, which doubles the frustration and wasted time.
3.	Airlines intervening due to delays:
The disorganization is so bad that airline staff have to step into the immigration lines to escort passengers directly to their gates. This is not only disruptive but also a clear sign of how unmanageable the airport’s system is.
4.	Plan for long delays:
Passengers are forced to budget at least two additional hours just to ensure they don’t miss their flights due to the airport’s inefficiency.

For an airport representing a major city like Ho Chi Minh, this level of mismanagement is unacceptable. It’s shocking that such a critical hub for travelers can’t operate with even basic efficiency. This experience is a stark contrast to other Asian airports that handle similar passenger volumes with ease.

If you can avoid this airport, I strongly recommend doing so. Personally, I am relieved to return to Thailand, where the airports are far better organized and offer a smooth, professional experience.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: David
text: L'aéroport m'a semblé agréable surtout quand on arrive de Bangkok BKK (trop grand) où j'ai décollé. Arrivé ce 25 décembre à 9h30 du matin. Au moins 60 (ou 90) min de file pour passer le contrôle des passeports alors que tous les guichets sont ouverts mais avec de longues files et après de longs vols de 14-15 heures, c'est fatiguant ... Ils feraient bien d'agrandir cette zone en ajoutant des guichets parce que c'est vraiment fatiguant pour tout ces gens qui viennent ici après de longs vols d'avion... Pour le reste c'est assez relax. Quand on arrive, c'est comme dans tous les aéroports, c'est à dire, avec les bureaux pour changer les différentes devises en "Dong" et une myriade de petits revendeurs de carte SIM qui sont prêts à vous expliquer avec le sourire le forfait parfaitement adapté à vos besoins en fonction de nombre de jours que vous aller rester... Il y a pas trop de stress. On sent une ambiance un peu relaxe. Assez étonnant d'ailleurs une fois qu'on prend le taxi qu'on découvre la ville qui elle est vraiment bouillonnante... Pour information, en tant que belge, j'ai dû prendre un visa (e-visa pour maximum 3 mois) pour rentrer par voie aérienne dans le pays. Coût 25 € une entrée ou 50 € pour double entrée. Je l'ai reçu après 3 jours ouvrables complet. Commandé dimanche soir et recu jeudi par email. Quand vous commandez le visa on vous demande entre autre la date d'entrée et la date de sortie. Comme je voulais acheter mon vol d'avion après avoir obtenu le visa, J'ai mentionné une date très proche, ainsi j'étais sûr d'arriver au Vietnam après la date d'entrée du visa et j'ai indiqué une date longue pour le retour. J'ai mis un hôtel fictif. On m'avait dit qu'ils allaient peut-être me demander la preuve d'un billet retour. J'aime voyager sans acheter à l'avance le retour. Au contrôle des passeports il ne m'a même pas demandé mon visa. Il ne m'a pas demandé de billet de retour. En fait, il m'a juste mis un cacher dans le passeport avec la date d'entrée et la date finale. Par contre, quand je suis rentré dans l'avion à Bruxelles avec la thai Airways (BRU-BKK-SGN) ils ont bien contrôlé mon visa juste avant mon embarquement et également pour d'autres voyageurs. Ils m'ont demandé si j'avais un retour et j'ai dit que j'allais revenir par la frontière terrestre et ils m'ont laissé embarquer. Ils m'ont dit d'ailleurs que la compagnie aérienne est responsable de cette vérification. En cas de problème, il paye toujours d'amende. J'espère que ça a pu vous aider.

🚩🚩 ALERT: READ THIS CAREFULLY 🚩🚩

I do NOT recommend this airport. If possible, please AVOID this airport at all cost. This is genuinely the worst airport I've ever been to.

The restaurants (even before security check) list all their prices in USD and when asking to pay in VND, the staff either refuses or uses an unfair exchange rate. Furthermore, the quality of the food and drinks is terrible. Old and spoiled food are being sold here. The prices of food is unfairly high. For example, at thr Burger King here, they ask 18 dollars for a small menu.

Shops are really weird and there are random stands with souvenirs etc everywhere. The staff of these little shops try to lure you in. Prices are extraordinary high and it's even shameless to ask such high prices.

Lastly, the airport is filthy. We have seen multiple cockroaches walking around the gates in the waiting lobbies. I can't put my head around this.

So once again, please AVOID this airport.

text: No issues during my departure from HCMC on a weekday early afternoon. There was no wait at the ticket counter to check in to my flight (VietJet) and get my boarding pass, immigration line took 30 minutes, then customs/security line took 20 minutes. Those lines looked about half capacity, so at peak times it could be longer, but I found the queueing system well organized and efficient (one line split into multiple stations). I have not been able to find a drinking fountain past security, so be prepared to buy water, but the bathrooms are clean and stocked. The airport vendors accept credit cards, and prices are listed in US $. It's more expensive than in the city, for example a 500ml bottle of water costs $1.50.

Update 2: no running water except 1 tap put of 6 taps in men’s toilet (international arrival) & no drinking water available as machine broken. Can I give half a star for an airport?

name: Quỳnh Thương Tống Thị
text:
Em là du học sinh Úc
Em có chuyến bay hãng vietjet lúc 1:30 sáng 26/12/2024 từ Melbourne úc về sân bay tân sân nhất sài gòn , nhưng em đã bị delay 4 tiếng , em vẫn happy chờ vì nhân viên sân bay ở Melbourne úc rất thân thiện, tiếp viên hàng không của chuyến vj082 cũng rất tốt ạ . Em chuyển Chuyến  về Hải phòng bị chuyển từ 9h sáng sang 9h đêm. Nhưng còn hơn khi là  xuống tới sân bay tân sân nhất. Thật thất vọng. Em không nghĩ một môi trường làm việc quốc tế anh chị nào cũng được học tập và được đào tạo mà lại trả lời khách hàng của mình với một thái độ cực kì thiếu tôn trong và vô văn hoá . Em cũng tự nhận em không biết lối đi . Nhưng em là con gái em cũng nhẹ nhàng hỏi  một chú mặc áo quân phục màu canh lam đậm tầm 40 tuổi. Vậy mà lại có thái độ cực kì thiếu tôn trọng và khó chịu. Tiếp đó là đến quầy chuyển chuyến nội địa , nhân biên cười đùa dùng điện thoại và phớt lờ câu hỏi của em . Em biết em còn ít tuổi nhưng mà em nghĩ em đi làm thái độ của em với khách hàng hơn achi và chú hôm nay em gặp ở sân bay này . Em nghĩ công việc của ai cũng khó khăn vất vả. Cũng biết không phải ac nhân viên nào cũng như vậy . Nhưng mà tôn trọng và trả lời câu hỏi của khách hàng là điều cơ bản mà ai cũng phải biết . Trong khi đó ac lại làm trong một môi trường có cả khách  quốc tế và ng dân của mình . Ng nước ngoài đặt chân xuống sân bay và gặp ac làm việc ở đây là đầu tiên . Ac là bộ mặt của đất nước Việt Nam . Em nghĩ được như vậy . Còn ac em không t cos nghĩ được như vậy không ạ Mong quản lý sân bay đọc được cmt
Này của em ạ
Thanks mọi người
Mong mọi người không phải găp những điều tồi tệ như em ạ …

2. Nhân viên thái độ kém văn hoá.
Tệ! Hôm qua cần tiễn bạn bay quốc tế lúc 9h tối. Một phần do trời tối, mình chạy xe máy theo bảng hướng dẫn đường vào ga quốc tế thì bị nhầm đường. Mình không hiểu tiêu chuẩn tuyển dụng, nhân phẩm nhân viên nhà nước/ sân bay quốc tế Việt Nam lại thấp như vậy sao?

name: Kyra Collier
text: Lost our baggage after we waited for all of our planes baggage and another’s to go through. After talking to multiple workers they said it has been expedited to our final location. After we had checked in ready for our connecting flight, they message us on WhatsApp that they have our baggage. I was trying to get food after I had had a 9 hour flight and waited in lines for both sides making it just about 12 and they said I could get back in without my passport as I’ve already checked in. After going in and receiving my luggage they make me wait at the gate where I was told I was allowed in without my passport. My partner had to run upstairs and through the halls to find my passport and run back to give it to security for my to make my layover when originally I had 6 hours I now had not even 1 not including having to board. Worst first experience overseas ever. Never coming to Vietnam again

text:
L’aeroporto internazionale Tan Son Nhat di Ho Chi Minh (ex Saigon) sorge praticamente a soli 6 km dal centro della città vietnamita. Costo di una corsa con Grab circa 4€.

L’aeroporto è piccolino rispetto al volume di traffico raggiunto e sebbene sia stato ammodernato, necessita di ampliamenti.

I servizi non sono eccelsi, i bagni non particolarmente puliti (non a livello di Bangkok) ma lo staff super gentile e tutti parlano inglese.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Ritthikiat Wongpan-ngam
text:
Domestic terminal

It’s fine

Flying via Vietjet to Da Nang

If you plan to bring only your carry-on, they are very strict with the weight limit and will weigh your stuff before you reach the security check point so be prepared for that. Mine was 1-2 kg over the limit so I have to pay 100,000 VND as fees luckily as the front staff was very helpful (kudos!)

Security check point are pretty swift, but you have to take your shoes off as well

Inside are filled with marked-up price local food, in the realm of around 100k+ VND and a bottle of water cost 20k. The environment inside is crowded so you can choose to go to the other gate that are less crowded

Smoking room inside the gate are provided for any smokers out there.

No free wifi at the airport so be careful of your roaming/local sims data before coming here.

Toilet are bearable, and I haven’t found any drinking water fountains yet.

Giving 4 stars due to the very helpful Vietjet counter staff that slash my fees of checked baggage down from 300k to 100k otherwise would give a review of the airport’s domestic terminal a 3

text:
開了眼界...
自行上網申請的電子簽證，日期打錯為2025，海關退回到辦理落地簽的地方，一個簽證官收走了護照,我問現在怎麼處理，是辦落地簽嗎？他跟我說你的簽證不對，要遣返回台北，我想起了網友說的金錢大法，於是掏出了1000台幣，他說more,我再掏第二張，他只是一直說more,這跟我在網上看到的有些不同，我直接問他到底要多少，他轉頭走開不理我，過了一陣子來了另一個簽證官，第一個簽證官就把我的護照交給第二個，第二個走過來跟我說要200美金，我說我沒有那麼多然後他就掉頭不理了，之後這個簽證官叫來了一個越南航空的空姐，我以為空姐是來協助我辦理簽證的，最後騙我簽下了自願被遣返的文件,我將文件的照片發給朋友,朋友才告訴我那個是自願被遣返的，我很生氣的跑到櫃檯問質問空姐說你為什麼要騙我，我要辦的是簽證為什麼要騙我簽下這個自願被遣返的文件

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Ziyuan Wei
text:
Tan Son Nhat is massively over capacity for its current size so it's really a massive roll of the dice - depending on whether your flight arrives in a busy time. If you're lucky, expect immigrations to maybe take 30 minutes or so. Otherwise, I'd reckon that the lines could easily stretch up to over an hour long.
Departures wise, there really isn't any reprieve; it's easily going to take upwards of an hour on a decent day. However, I did see the staff allowing some passengers who were going to miss their flights cut to the front so keep that in mind if you ever find yourself in a sticky situation.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Irene Eng
text:
Tan Son Nhat International Airport / Korean Airline is the first airport that made me to check my carryon, that would set off a troublesome chain reaction in Saigon's SGN.

How did I leave Saigon, or about to leave.

It’s the first time ever that an airline made us to check in our carryons: over weight by 2kg. One of it has a charger, which will cause problem soon.

Customs. The agent asked for my eVisa, on exit the country. I always throw away unnecessary items, including used papers. As something unusual this time, I’ve both eVisas inside my passport. Before handing him my passport, I took the two folded papers out. Only when he asked me, I thought to myself, good heaven that we kept them, this time. So I handed him both. He looked at them and returned the first one to me. ... SO I'd think if I threw away my eVisa, the likelihood is, pay more to exit?!

After clearing the customs, I walked pass the booth, toward the security check. A woman was directing traffic, right after the customs. Some go left and some go right. She mentioned me to go right. But I waited for my spoons to clear the customs. When she saw me standing there, she barked in language I don’t understand. But after I pointed to my spouse, she was ok with it.
Then my spouse ISN'T ok.

The same agent demanded his eVisa and of course he doesn’t have it. So he was pulled. Of course he was sweating … after nearly ten minutes, little Irene suddenly thought, oh, maybe he needed his eVisa and that piece of paper the agent returned to me, was his eVisa or exit visa - thinking Casablanca.

For that short period of time, I did feel unease. We could see each other, separated by the bank of customs booths. I was inside and he was out...

We finally reunited, to go through the security check. He was cleared. And me, NOT. The problem? My checked in luggage has a problem, and they chase me down or find me.
Someone led me out of the security check point. Being told where to stand or what to do with sign language (or I call it) isn’t too fun. Seeing the green uniform, I told myself, this is no joke. So, when a stocky agent in the green uniform, used his finger to make a circle motion, and I went it. Soon a Korean Air agent came to collect me. The stocky asked for my passport - nice of him, so I don’t have to clear it again.
KA girl: you’ve battery in your suitcase.
Me: “no, I don’t.” Thinking all electronics, which is my iPhone is WITH me.
She said a bit more, I chose not to answer. Then she led me to a tiny shallow room: it isn’t a body check place or torture chamber -:). I took a pix of it. A man jumped out and barked something, I'm sure it's NO PHOTO. I put up my two arms as surrender. He said no more, and took no action. I kept the photo. I went into the tiny room. A worker pulled up my carryon, (wondering how did they know so precisely ...). “Battery” or I think he said battery. Without saying a word, I pulled out the charger …

It’s all easy peacy for the security check. Although they did make me to take off my shoes, scarf …
Reunited with my spoons again.
…

By now, I'm pretty spent, have no desire to go to a lounge. I just wanted to wait by the gate and leave. As I was waiting, I remembered the hotel or our tour manager insisted to pack me a breakfast bag. AND when I looked into the bag, I see a bottle of water! This is the only airport that let me to bring a bottle of water, unknowingly, because the hotel packed the bag.

Oh well. It’s 6:51am, the morning has just began.

text:
Tân Sơn Nhất Airport Infrastructure Status:
While Tân Sơn Nhất Airport maintains its position as a vital transportation hub, there is room for enhancement to fully align with modern standards. The existing infrastructure, though functional, reflects a sense of historical character that could benefit from a more cohesive and contemporary architectural design. Logistics operations, both inside and outside the terminal, present opportunities for optimization to improve efficiency and passenger experience. On a positive note, the pricing of food and beverage services has seen noticeable improvement, thanks to effective oversight from state regulatory agencies, ensuring better value for travelers.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: うれしいひととき
text:
７月に行きました。出口を出て右側の１番手前の両替所ではこちらが見てないと思って最初に出してきたお札から数枚抜き取ります。

後で計算し直したら千円以上少なかったのと領収書をよこしませんでした。

細かい数えにくいお札ばかりたくさん出してきて目の前で数えてほらあってるでしょという素振りでそれを鵜呑みにしてその場を離れましたが騙されていました。

後の祭りです。

空港の外にあるバーガーキングはハンバーガーは１０００円ぐらいで日本で食べるより遥かに高いです。

空港のお土産のブランドチョコレートは高くて不味くて量が少なくてブルームで真っ白でした。

マクドナルドはちょっと離れています。
空港出てすぐのバスの運転手は昼ごはんを食べていて休憩なんだから仕事をしないという態度でずっとドアを開けてくれませんでした。見かねて同じ番号のバスの係員が開けなさいよみんな待ってるでしょって大声で言ってもドアは開けずに１人バスの中でずっと食事をしていました。
食べ終わったらやっとドアを開けてお前らマスクをしろと圧をかけてきます。
そんなバスもありますがバスで行けるところはバスで行ったほうがものすごく安いです。スーツケースが大きいと１人分の料金をプラスされます。それでも安いです。

ブザーがあってもなくても降りる時が近づいたら前もってドアの前に立って降りる意思表示を係員に見せなければブザーを押しても降りれないこともあります。
バスで行きにくいところはグラブでいきましょう。空港の前の客引きタクシーは絶対に乗らないほうがいいです。

あと空港のトイレにはよく蚊がいるので気をつけて下さい。毒が多いのか刺されて異常に腫れ上がりました。
ベトナムで売っている虫よけはたくさんかけたのに足元に蚊がうろちょろしてたので全く効きませんでした。自国のスプレーを持参したほうがいいです。

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Trang Minh Hà (Bryan Trang)
text: Tan Son Nhat International Airport, a bustling hub of activity, serves as the vibrant gateway to Ho Chi Minh City.  As the largest airport in Vietnam, it welcomes travelers from across the globe, offering a glimpse into the country's rich culture and warm hospitality. The modern terminals, adorned with sleek architecture and efficient facilities, ensure a smooth and pleasant journey for passengers.  Beyond its functional purpose, the airport echoes with stories of reunions, new beginnings, and exciting adventures. Whether you're arriving or departing, Tan Son Nhat International Airport leaves a lasting impression, symbolizing the spirit of Vietnam's dynamic growth and connection to the world.

title: Cảng hàng không quốc tế Tân Sơn Nhất
name: Arijit Sarkar
text:
The airport ranks among the worst in the world. And one can clearly see why. Two experiences through departures and arrivals reinforce this.
Arrivals - the longest queues you’ll ever see at immigration. And the agents take their time. Plan on easily spending upwards of 1 hrs in that queue on a good day, 1.5 hrs is more realistic. There is zero queue management. Every time they open a new counter, there is a mad stampede. They even sell SIM cards at the start of the queue.
Departures - same story with immigration and security. Once in, there isn’t much by way of sitting areas or recreational areas. A few eateries but not really any place to relax and wait other than the boarding gates themselves.
Overall though, the airport is basically functional and staff are nice and courteous. The airport planning and administration is abysmal.

いろんな国の空港を利用してきて、ここが一番レベル低いと感じました。今どき無料Wi-Fiがないとかありえないし、入管審査は何の質問もなくパスポートと顔を見比べるだけ。長蛇の列ができていても各審査官はのんびり、さばくスタッフもいない。出国する際は、リュックに飲みかけのペットボトルを入れたままにしていたのに気づかず手荷物検査を通過できました。金属探知機に引っかかった時もボディチェックは形だけでどこもしっかり触ってませんでした。何でも持ち込みし放題だし、犯罪者も通過し放題だと思います。
誰かのブログで書いてあった「世界一やる気のない空港」というのは本当でした。

name: Peter Hodgson
text: I give zero Stars if possible. Flew in with our US$25 visas that had to be done twice and paid twice because of the pathetic website. Wife goes to immigration official with passport and Visa and he goes your visa is out of date so you need to go over to the main office. Lots of passengers lined up and all miraculously with out of date visas. She gets told we can help you out by supplying and instant Visa for US$250 cash only. We will get a travel agent in to help you through the process. They had 4 agents doing instant Visas for a percentage of passengers off every arriving plane, not a bad earn eh! I go to the agent we don't have US$ and only AUD$300 and he goes that'll do so we reluctantly paid and kept quiet as we had a departing flight 2 days later so check your visas very very carefully. They obviously don't want tourists back so happy to rip off a percentage of unsuspecting tourists.

name: Isuru Dissanayake
text: Visited this airport during my visit to Saigon. This is the main airport that serves the travel needs of the people who are visiting Ho Chi Minh City. The arrival experience was horrendous.
